Some scholars interpret "Shitomi" as derived from the verb "shitomu," meaning "to soak in water" or "to get wet." Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 336. == Ōkōchi Shrine == Although its structure is separate, Ōkōchi Shrine is located within the same fence as Shitomi Shrine.Ise Bunka Sha (2008): p. 54. It ranks 8th among the 16 auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代}}, Ōkōchi Shrine fell into disrepair and later became a component of Kami Shrine as the "Mountain Shrine."Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 950. The current pronunciation of the shrine's name is "Ōkōchi," but historical documents record readings such as "Ōkahachi" and "Ōgafuchi."Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 354. == Uchikake Shrine == Uchikake Shrine ranks 4th among the eight subsidiary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 1, pp. 13–15. After falling into disrepair during the Sengoku period, it was absorbed into Kami Shrine as "Parallel Shrine" (formerly "Hachioji Shrine"), maintaining its original deity.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 948. == Kami Shrine == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Kami Shrine (上社) |image = [[File:Kami no Shrine, Ise.jpg|250px]] |location = {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, Tsujikuru 1-chome 13 |coordinates = {{coord|34|29|22.3|N|136|41|21.0|E}} |Map = {{location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=29|lat_sec=22.3|lon_deg=136|lon_min=41|lon_sec=21.0|label=Kami Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} |deity = {{ill|Kukunochi|ja|久久能智神}}, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祗神}}, {{ill|Haniyasu|ja|埴安神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂神}}, and others |Rank = Former Village Shrine (旧村社) |Founded = After the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代}} |Main hall = |Alternate names = |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = July 15 |Rituals = {{ill|Onzu-shinji (Head Ritual)|ja|御頭神事}} (January 15) }} Kami Shrine is the local {{ill|ujigami|ja|氏神}}, serving the districts of Nakajima, Tsuji Kuru, Futamata, and Uraguchi. Its treasures include a lion head for {{ill|shishimai|ja|獅子舞}} performances and a sword donated in 1721.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 955. The precincts feature additional shrines such as Inari Shrine, Uegami Shrine dedicated to Sugawara no Michizane, and Kushitama Shrine enshrining Isetsuhiko and Isetsuhime deities, as well as remote worship sites for other shrines.Ise-Shima Kirari 1000 (2013). === Merged Shrines === Kami Shrine absorbed 13 other shrines in 1909.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, pp. 949. == Worship and Festivals == Shitomi Shrine conducts annual festivals, including the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}}, {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭}}, {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|ja|神嘗祭}}, and {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}}.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 343, 355. {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Shitomi Shrine & Ōkōchi Shrine |image = [[File:Shitomi & Okouchi Shrine.jpg|250px]] |location = {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, Tsujikuru 1-chome 13 |Latitude = 34 |Latitude minutes = 29 |Latitude seconds = 23.1 |Longitude = 136 |Longitude minutes = 41 |Longitude seconds = 22.1 |Map = Japan Mie |deity = Shitomi Shrine: {{ill|Kukunochi|ja|久久能智神}}
Ōkōchi Shrine: {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祗神}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (Minor)
{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮}} Auxiliary Shrine (Sessha) |Founded = Before {{ill|Enryaku era|ja|延暦}} 23 (804 CE) |Main hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} |Alternate names = |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October 19) |Rituals = {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}}, {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭}}, and others }} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[ja:志等美神社]] [[ja:大河内神社]] [[ja:上社]] '''Shitomi Shrine''' (志等美神社) is a shrine in Japan associated with the {{ill|Outer Shrine|ja|外宮}} of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. This article also covers the {{ill|Ōkōchi Shrine|ja|大河内神社}}, which shares the same grounds as Shitomi Shrine, as well as the {{ill|Uchikake Shrine|ja|打懸神社}} and the former village shrine known as {{ill|Kami Shrine (Ise)|ja|上社}}, which encompasses all three shrines within its precincts. Among the 125 shrines affiliated with Ise Grand Shrine, only Shitomi, Ōkōchi, and Uchikake shrines hold recognized ranks in the {{ill|Shinkai (divine rank)|ja|神階|Shinkai (divine rank)=Shinkai (divine rank)|lt=Shinto shrine ranking system}}.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 342, 355. == Overview == Shitomi Shrine is located at Tsuji Kuru 1-13, Ise City, Mie Prefecture. The precincts are under the jurisdiction of Kami Shrine, with Shitomi, Ōkōchi, and Uchikake shrines positioned at the right side of Kami Shrine's eastern entrance.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 949. The total area of the precincts is approximately 700 tsubo (about 2,314 square meters), of which 406 square meters are occupied by Shitomi, Ōkōchi, and Uchikake shrines.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 343, 356. Shitomi and Ōkōchi shrines share the same fence, facing south, and feature {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} architecture with wooden plank roofs. These shrines do not have offering boxes. == Shitomi Shrine == Shitomi Shrine ranks 7th among the 16 auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 1, pp. 7, 11–12. Historical records also refer to it as "Shitomi-no-Iba Shrine," with "Shitomi-no-Iba" being the name of the surrounding wilderness.Ise Bunka Sha (2008): p. 53. Some scholars interpret "Shitomi" as derived from the verb "shitomu," meaning "to soak in water" or "to get wet." Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 336. == Ōkōchi Shrine == Although its structure is separate, Ōkōchi Shrine is located within the same fence as Shitomi Shrine.Ise Bunka Sha (2008): p. 54. It ranks 8th among the 16 auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代}}, Ōkōchi Shrine fell into disrepair and later became a component of Kami Shrine as the "Mountain Shrine."Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 950. The current pronunciation of the shrine's name is "Ōkōchi," but historical documents record readings such as "Ōkahachi" and "Ōgafuchi."Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 354. == Uchikake Shrine == Uchikake Shrine ranks 4th among the eight subsidiary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 1, pp. 13–15. After falling into disrepair during the Sengoku period, it was absorbed into Kami Shrine as "Parallel Shrine" (formerly "Hachioji Shrine"), maintaining its original deity.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 948. == Kami Shrine == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Kami Shrine (上社) |image = [[File:Kami no Shrine, Ise.jpg|250px]] |location = {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, Tsujikuru 1-chome 13 |coordinates = {{coord|34|29|22.3|N|136|41|21.0|E}} |Map = {{location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=29|lat_sec=22.3|lon_deg=136|lon_min=41|lon_sec=21.0|label=Kami Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} |deity = {{ill|Kukunochi|ja|久久能智神}}, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祗神}}, {{ill|Haniyasu|ja|埴安神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂神}}, and others |Rank = Former Village Shrine (旧村社) |Founded = After the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代}} |Main hall = |Alternate names = |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = July 15 |Rituals = {{ill|Onzu-shinji (Head Ritual)|ja|御頭神事}} (January 15) }} Kami Shrine is the local {{ill|ujigami|ja|氏神}}, serving the districts of Nakajima, Tsuji Kuru, Futamata, and Uraguchi. Its treasures include a lion head for {{ill|shishimai|ja|獅子舞}} performances and a sword donated in 1721.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 955. The precincts feature additional shrines such as Inari Shrine, Uegami Shrine dedicated to Sugawara no Michizane, and Kushitama Shrine enshrining Isetsuhiko and Isetsuhime deities, as well as remote worship sites for other shrines.Ise-Shima Kirari 1000 (2013). === Merged Shrines === Kami Shrine absorbed 13 other shrines in 1909.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, pp. 949. == Worship and Festivals == Shitomi Shrine conducts annual festivals, including the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}}, {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭}}, {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|ja|神嘗祭}}, and {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}}.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 343, 355. Shitomi and Ōkōchi shrines share the same fence, facing south, and feature {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} architecture with wooden plank roofs. These shrines do not have offering boxes. == Shitomi Shrine == Shitomi Shrine ranks 7th among the 16 auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 1, pp. 7, 11–12. Historical records also refer to it as "Shitomi-no-Iba Shrine," with "Shitomi-no-Iba" being the name of the surrounding wilderness.Ise Bunka Sha (2008): p. 53. Some scholars interpret "Shitomi" as derived from the verb "shitomu," meaning "to soak in water" or "to get wet." Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 336. == Ōkōchi Shrine == Although its structure is separate, Ōkōchi Shrine is located within the same fence as Shitomi Shrine.Ise Bunka Sha (2008): p. 54. It ranks 8th among the 16 auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代}}, Ōkōchi Shrine fell into disrepair and later became a component of Kami Shrine as the "Mountain Shrine."Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 950. The current pronunciation of the shrine's name is "Ōkōchi," but historical documents record readings such as "Ōkahachi" and "Ōgafuchi."Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 354. == Uchikake Shrine == Uchikake Shrine ranks 4th among the eight subsidiary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 1, pp. 13–15. After falling into disrepair during the Sengoku period, it was absorbed into Kami Shrine as "Parallel Shrine" (formerly "Hachioji Shrine"), maintaining its original deity.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 948. == Kami Shrine == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Kami Shrine (上社) |image = [[File:Kami no Shrine, Ise.jpg|250px]] |location = {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, Tsujikuru 1-chome 13 |coordinates = {{coord|34|29|22.3|N|136|41|21.0|E}} |Map = {{location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=29|lat_sec=22.3|lon_deg=136|lon_min=41|lon_sec=21.0|label=Kami Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} |deity = {{ill|Kukunochi|ja|久久能智神}}, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祗神}}, {{ill|Haniyasu|ja|埴安神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂神}}, and others |Rank = Former Village Shrine (旧村社) |Founded = After the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代}} |Main hall = |Alternate names = |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = July 15 |Rituals = {{ill|Onzu-shinji (Head Ritual)|ja|御頭神事}} (January 15) }} Kami Shrine is the local {{ill|ujigami|ja|氏神}}, serving the districts of Nakajima, Tsuji Kuru, Futamata, and Uraguchi. Its treasures include a lion head for {{ill|shishimai|ja|獅子舞}} performances and a sword donated in 1721.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 955. The precincts feature additional shrines such as Inari Shrine, Uegami Shrine dedicated to Sugawara no Michizane, and Kushitama Shrine enshrining Isetsuhiko and Isetsuhime deities, as well as remote worship sites for other shrines.Ise-Shima Kirari 1000 (2013). === Merged Shrines === Kami Shrine absorbed 13 other shrines in 1909.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, pp. 949. == Worship and Festivals == Shitomi Shrine conducts annual festivals, including the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}}, {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭}}, {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|ja|神嘗祭}}, and {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}}.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 343, 355. This article also covers the {{ill|Ōkōchi Shrine|ja|大河内神社}}, which shares the same grounds as Shitomi Shrine, as well as the {{ill|Uchikake Shrine|ja|打懸神社}} and the former village shrine known as {{ill|Kami Shrine (Ise)|ja|上社}}, which encompasses all three shrines within its precincts. Among the 125 shrines affiliated with Ise Grand Shrine, only Shitomi, Ōkōchi, and Uchikake shrines hold recognized ranks in the {{ill|Shinkai (divine rank)|ja|神階|Shinkai (divine rank)=Shinkai (divine rank)|lt=Shinto shrine ranking system}}.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 342, 355. == Overview == Shitomi Shrine is located at Tsuji Kuru 1-13, Ise City, Mie Prefecture. The precincts are under the jurisdiction of Kami Shrine, with Shitomi, Ōkōchi, and Uchikake shrines positioned at the right side of Kami Shrine's eastern entrance.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 949. The total area of the precincts is approximately 700 tsubo (about 2,314 square meters), of which 406 square meters are occupied by Shitomi, Ōkōchi, and Uchikake shrines.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 343, 356. Shitomi and Ōkōchi shrines share the same fence, facing south, and feature {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} architecture with wooden plank roofs. These shrines do not have offering boxes. == Shitomi Shrine == Shitomi Shrine ranks 7th among the 16 auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 1, pp. 7, 11–12. Historical records also refer to it as "Shitomi-no-Iba Shrine," with "Shitomi-no-Iba" being the name of the surrounding wilderness.Ise Bunka Sha (2008): p. 53. Some scholars interpret "Shitomi" as derived from the verb "shitomu," meaning "to soak in water" or "to get wet." Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 336. == Ōkōchi Shrine == Although its structure is separate, Ōkōchi Shrine is located within the same fence as Shitomi Shrine.Ise Bunka Sha (2008): p. 54. It ranks 8th among the 16 auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代}}, Ōkōchi Shrine fell into disrepair and later became a component of Kami Shrine as the "Mountain Shrine."Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 950. The current pronunciation of the shrine's name is "Ōkōchi," but historical documents record readings such as "Ōkahachi" and "Ōgafuchi."Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 354. == Uchikake Shrine == Uchikake Shrine ranks 4th among the eight subsidiary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 1, pp. 13–15. The precincts feature additional shrines such as Inari Shrine, Uegami Shrine dedicated to Sugawara no Michizane, and Kushitama Shrine enshrining Isetsuhiko and Isetsuhime deities, as well as remote worship sites for other shrines.Ise-Shima Kirari 1000 (2013). === Merged Shrines === Kami Shrine absorbed 13 other shrines in 1909.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, pp. 949. == Worship and Festivals == Shitomi Shrine conducts annual festivals, including the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}}, {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭}}, {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|ja|神嘗祭}}, and {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}}.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 343, 355. {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮}} Auxiliary Shrine (Sessha) |Founded = Before {{ill|Enryaku era|ja|延暦}} 23 (804 CE) |Main hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} |Alternate names = |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October 19) |Rituals = {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}}, {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭}}, and others }} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[ja:志等美神社]] [[ja:大河内神社]] [[ja:上社]] '''Shitomi Shrine''' (志等美神社) is a shrine in Japan associated with the {{ill|Outer Shrine|ja|外宮}} of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. This article also covers the {{ill|Ōkōchi Shrine|ja|大河内神社}}, which shares the same grounds as Shitomi Shrine, as well as the {{ill|Uchikake Shrine|ja|打懸神社}} and the former village shrine known as {{ill|Kami Shrine (Ise)|ja|上社}}, which encompasses all three shrines within its precincts. Among the 125 shrines affiliated with Ise Grand Shrine, only Shitomi, Ōkōchi, and Uchikake shrines hold recognized ranks in the {{ill|Shinkai (divine rank)|ja|神階|Shinkai (divine rank)=Shinkai (divine rank)|lt=Shinto shrine ranking system}}.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 342, 355. == Overview == Shitomi Shrine is located at Tsuji Kuru 1-13, Ise City, Mie Prefecture. The precincts are under the jurisdiction of Kami Shrine, with Shitomi, Ōkōchi, and Uchikake shrines positioned at the right side of Kami Shrine's eastern entrance.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 949. The total area of the precincts is approximately 700 tsubo (about 2,314 square meters), of which 406 square meters are occupied by Shitomi, Ōkōchi, and Uchikake shrines.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 343, 356. Shitomi and Ōkōchi shrines share the same fence, facing south, and feature {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} architecture with wooden plank roofs. These shrines do not have offering boxes. == Shitomi Shrine == Shitomi Shrine ranks 7th among the 16 auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 1, pp. 7, 11–12. Historical records also refer to it as "Shitomi-no-Iba Shrine," with "Shitomi-no-Iba" being the name of the surrounding wilderness.Ise Bunka Sha (2008): p. 53. Some scholars interpret "Shitomi" as derived from the verb "shitomu," meaning "to soak in water" or "to get wet." Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 336. == Ōkōchi Shrine == Although its structure is separate, Ōkōchi Shrine is located within the same fence as Shitomi Shrine.Ise Bunka Sha (2008): p. 54. It ranks 8th among the 16 auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代}}, Ōkōchi Shrine fell into disrepair and later became a component of Kami Shrine as the "Mountain Shrine."Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 950. The current pronunciation of the shrine's name is "Ōkōchi," but historical documents record readings such as "Ōkahachi" and "Ōgafuchi."Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 354. == Uchikake Shrine == Uchikake Shrine ranks 4th among the eight subsidiary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 1, pp. 13–15. After falling into disrepair during the Sengoku period, it was absorbed into Kami Shrine as "Parallel Shrine" (formerly "Hachioji Shrine"), maintaining its original deity.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 948. == Kami Shrine == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Kami Shrine (上社) |image = [[File:Kami no Shrine, Ise.jpg|250px]] |location = {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, Tsujikuru 1-chome 13 |coordinates = {{coord|34|29|22.3|N|136|41|21.0|E}} |Map = {{location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=29|lat_sec=22.3|lon_deg=136|lon_min=41|lon_sec=21.0|label=Kami Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} |deity = {{ill|Kukunochi|ja|久久能智神}}, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祗神}}, {{ill|Haniyasu|ja|埴安神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂神}}, and others |Rank = Former Village Shrine (旧村社) |Founded = After the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代}} |Main hall = |Alternate names = |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = July 15 |Rituals = {{ill|Onzu-shinji (Head Ritual)|ja|御頭神事}} (January 15) }} Kami Shrine is the local {{ill|ujigami|ja|氏神}}, serving the districts of Nakajima, Tsuji Kuru, Futamata, and Uraguchi. Its treasures include a lion head for {{ill|shishimai|ja|獅子舞}} performances and a sword donated in 1721.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 955. The precincts feature additional shrines such as Inari Shrine, Uegami Shrine dedicated to Sugawara no Michizane, and Kushitama Shrine enshrining Isetsuhiko and Isetsuhime deities, as well as remote worship sites for other shrines.Ise-Shima Kirari 1000 (2013). === Merged Shrines === Kami Shrine absorbed 13 other shrines in 1909.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, pp. 949. == Worship and Festivals == Shitomi Shrine conducts annual festivals, including the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}}, {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭}}, {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|ja|神嘗祭}}, and {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}}.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 343, 355. Kami Shrine's events include the {{ill|Mitō-shinji|ja|御頭神事}} on January 15 and its annual festival on July 15. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Ujiyamada City Hall (1929). *Ujiyamada City History*. Vols. 1–2. * Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990). *Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku: Vol. 6, Tōkaidō 1*. Kōgakkan University Press. * Ise Bunka Sha (2008). *O-Ise-san 125 Shrine Pilgrimage*. Ise Bunka Sha. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0605.html Shitomi Shrine] - Jingu Kaikan. * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0606.html Ōkōchi Shrine] - Jingu Kaikan. * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0604.html Uchikake Shrine] - Jingu Kaikan. {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Shitomi Shrine & Ōkōchi Shrine |image = [[File:Shitomi & Okouchi Shrine.jpg|250px]] |location = {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, Tsujikuru 1-chome 13 |Latitude = 34 |Latitude minutes = 29 |Latitude seconds = 23.1 |Longitude = 136 |Longitude minutes = 41 |Longitude seconds = 22.1 |Map = Japan Mie |deity = Shitomi Shrine: {{ill|Kukunochi|ja|久久能智神}}
Ōkōchi Shrine: {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祗神}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (Minor)
{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮}} Auxiliary Shrine (Sessha) |Founded = Before {{ill|Enryaku era|ja|延暦}} 23 (804 CE) |Main hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} |Alternate names = |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October 19) |Rituals = {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}}, {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭}}, and others }} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[ja:志等美神社]] [[ja:大河内神社]] [[ja:上社]] '''Shitomi Shrine''' (志等美神社) is a shrine in Japan associated with the {{ill|Outer Shrine|ja|外宮}} of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. This article also covers the {{ill|Ōkōchi Shrine|ja|大河内神社}}, which shares the same grounds as Shitomi Shrine, as well as the {{ill|Uchikake Shrine|ja|打懸神社}} and the former village shrine known as {{ill|Kami Shrine (Ise)|ja|上社}}, which encompasses all three shrines within its precincts. Among the 125 shrines affiliated with Ise Grand Shrine, only Shitomi, Ōkōchi, and Uchikake shrines hold recognized ranks in the {{ill|Shinkai (divine rank)|ja|神階|Shinkai (divine rank)=Shinkai (divine rank)|lt=Shinto shrine ranking system}}.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 342, 355. == Overview == Shitomi Shrine is located at Tsuji Kuru 1-13, Ise City, Mie Prefecture. The precincts are under the jurisdiction of Kami Shrine, with Shitomi, Ōkōchi, and Uchikake shrines positioned at the right side of Kami Shrine's eastern entrance.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 949. The total area of the precincts is approximately 700 tsubo (about 2,314 square meters), of which 406 square meters are occupied by Shitomi, Ōkōchi, and Uchikake shrines.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 343, 356. Shitomi and Ōkōchi shrines share the same fence, facing south, and feature {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} architecture with wooden plank roofs. These shrines do not have offering boxes. == Shitomi Shrine == Shitomi Shrine ranks 7th among the 16 auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 1, pp. 7, 11–12. Historical records also refer to it as "Shitomi-no-Iba Shrine," with "Shitomi-no-Iba" being the name of the surrounding wilderness.Ise Bunka Sha (2008): p. 53. Some scholars interpret "Shitomi" as derived from the verb "shitomu," meaning "to soak in water" or "to get wet." Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 336. == Ōkōchi Shrine == Although its structure is separate, Ōkōchi Shrine is located within the same fence as Shitomi Shrine.Ise Bunka Sha (2008): p. 54. It ranks 8th among the 16 auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代}}, Ōkōchi Shrine fell into disrepair and later became a component of Kami Shrine as the "Mountain Shrine."Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 950. The current pronunciation of the shrine's name is "Ōkōchi," but historical documents record readings such as "Ōkahachi" and "Ōgafuchi."Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 354. == Uchikake Shrine == Uchikake Shrine ranks 4th among the eight subsidiary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 1, pp. 13–15. After falling into disrepair during the Sengoku period, it was absorbed into Kami Shrine as "Parallel Shrine" (formerly "Hachioji Shrine"), maintaining its original deity.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 948. == Kami Shrine == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Kami Shrine (上社) |image = [[File:Kami no Shrine, Ise.jpg|250px]] |location = {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, Tsujikuru 1-chome 13 |coordinates = {{coord|34|29|22.3|N|136|41|21.0|E}} |Map = {{location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=29|lat_sec=22.3|lon_deg=136|lon_min=41|lon_sec=21.0|label=Kami Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} |deity = {{ill|Kukunochi|ja|久久能智神}}, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祗神}}, {{ill|Haniyasu|ja|埴安神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂神}}, and others |Rank = Former Village Shrine (旧村社) |Founded = After the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代}} |Main hall = |Alternate names = |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = July 15 |Rituals = {{ill|Onzu-shinji (Head Ritual)|ja|御頭神事}} (January 15) }} Kami Shrine is the local {{ill|ujigami|ja|氏神}}, serving the districts of Nakajima, Tsuji Kuru, Futamata, and Uraguchi. Its treasures include a lion head for {{ill|shishimai|ja|獅子舞}} performances and a sword donated in 1721.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 955. The precincts feature additional shrines such as Inari Shrine, Uegami Shrine dedicated to Sugawara no Michizane, and Kushitama Shrine enshrining Isetsuhiko and Isetsuhime deities, as well as remote worship sites for other shrines.Ise-Shima Kirari 1000 (2013). === Merged Shrines === Kami Shrine absorbed 13 other shrines in 1909.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, pp. 949. == Worship and Festivals == Shitomi Shrine conducts annual festivals, including the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}}, {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭}}, {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|ja|神嘗祭}}, and {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}}.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 343, 355. Kami Shrine's events include the {{ill|Mitō-shinji|ja|御頭神事}} on January 15 and its annual festival on July 15. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Ujiyamada City Hall (1929). *Ujiyamada City History*. Vols. 1–2. * Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990). *Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku: Vol. 6, Tōkaidō 1*. Kōgakkan University Press. * Ise Bunka Sha (2008). *O-Ise-san 125 Shrine Pilgrimage*. Ise Bunka Sha. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0605.html Shitomi Shrine] - Jingu Kaikan. * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0606.html Ōkōchi Shrine] - Jingu Kaikan. * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0604.html Uchikake Shrine] - Jingu Kaikan. {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Shitomi Shrine & Ōkōchi Shrine |image = [[File:Shitomi & Okouchi Shrine.jpg|250px]] |location = {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, Tsujikuru 1-chome 13 |Latitude = 34 |Latitude minutes = 29 |Latitude seconds = 23.1 |Longitude = 136 |Longitude minutes = 41 |Longitude seconds = 22.1 |Map = Japan Mie |deity = Shitomi Shrine: {{ill|Kukunochi|ja|久久能智神}}
Ōkōchi Shrine: {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祗神}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (Minor)
{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮}} Auxiliary Shrine (Sessha) |Founded = Before {{ill|Enryaku era|ja|延暦}} 23 (804 CE) |Main hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} |Alternate names = |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October 19) |Rituals = {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}}, {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭}}, and others }} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[ja:志等美神社]] [[ja:大河内神社]] [[ja:上社]] '''Shitomi Shrine''' (志等美神社) is a shrine in Japan associated with the {{ill|Outer Shrine|ja|外宮}} of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. This article also covers the {{ill|Ōkōchi Shrine|ja|大河内神社}}, which shares the same grounds as Shitomi Shrine, as well as the {{ill|Uchikake Shrine|ja|打懸神社}} and the former village shrine known as {{ill|Kami Shrine (Ise)|ja|上社}}, which encompasses all three shrines within its precincts. Among the 125 shrines affiliated with Ise Grand Shrine, only Shitomi, Ōkōchi, and Uchikake shrines hold recognized ranks in the {{ill|Shinkai (divine rank)|ja|神階|Shinkai (divine rank)=Shinkai (divine rank)|lt=Shinto shrine ranking system}}.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 342, 355. == Overview == Shitomi Shrine is located at Tsuji Kuru 1-13, Ise City, Mie Prefecture. The precincts are under the jurisdiction of Kami Shrine, with Shitomi, Ōkōchi, and Uchikake shrines positioned at the right side of Kami Shrine's eastern entrance.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 949. The total area of the precincts is approximately 700 tsubo (about 2,314 square meters), of which 406 square meters are occupied by Shitomi, Ōkōchi, and Uchikake shrines.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 343, 356. Shitomi and Ōkōchi shrines share the same fence, facing south, and feature {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} architecture with wooden plank roofs. These shrines do not have offering boxes. == Shitomi Shrine == Shitomi Shrine ranks 7th among the 16 auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 1, pp. 7, 11–12. Historical records also refer to it as "Shitomi-no-Iba Shrine," with "Shitomi-no-Iba" being the name of the surrounding wilderness.Ise Bunka Sha (2008): p. 53. Some scholars interpret "Shitomi" as derived from the verb "shitomu," meaning "to soak in water" or "to get wet." Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 336. == Ōkōchi Shrine == Although its structure is separate, Ōkōchi Shrine is located within the same fence as Shitomi Shrine.Ise Bunka Sha (2008): p. 54. It ranks 8th among the 16 auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代}}, Ōkōchi Shrine fell into disrepair and later became a component of Kami Shrine as the "Mountain Shrine."Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 950. The current pronunciation of the shrine's name is "Ōkōchi," but historical documents record readings such as "Ōkahachi" and "Ōgafuchi."Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 354. == Uchikake Shrine == Uchikake Shrine ranks 4th among the eight subsidiary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 1, pp. 13–15. After falling into disrepair during the Sengoku period, it was absorbed into Kami Shrine as "Parallel Shrine" (formerly "Hachioji Shrine"), maintaining its original deity.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 948. == Kami Shrine == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Kami Shrine (上社) |image = [[File:Kami no Shrine, Ise.jpg|250px]] |location = {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, Tsujikuru 1-chome 13 |coordinates = {{coord|34|29|22.3|N|136|41|21.0|E}} |Map = {{location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=29|lat_sec=22.3|lon_deg=136|lon_min=41|lon_sec=21.0|label=Kami Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} |deity = {{ill|Kukunochi|ja|久久能智神}}, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祗神}}, {{ill|Haniyasu|ja|埴安神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂神}}, and others |Rank = Former Village Shrine (旧村社) |Founded = After the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代}} |Main hall = |Alternate names = |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = July 15 |Rituals = {{ill|Onzu-shinji (Head Ritual)|ja|御頭神事}} (January 15) }} Kami Shrine is the local {{ill|ujigami|ja|氏神}}, serving the districts of Nakajima, Tsuji Kuru, Futamata, and Uraguchi. Its treasures include a lion head for {{ill|shishimai|ja|獅子舞}} performances and a sword donated in 1721.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 955. The precincts feature additional shrines such as Inari Shrine, Uegami Shrine dedicated to Sugawara no Michizane, and Kushitama Shrine enshrining Isetsuhiko and Isetsuhime deities, as well as remote worship sites for other shrines.Ise-Shima Kirari 1000 (2013). === Merged Shrines === Kami Shrine absorbed 13 other shrines in 1909.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, pp. 949. == Worship and Festivals == Shitomi Shrine conducts annual festivals, including the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}}, {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭}}, {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|ja|神嘗祭}}, and {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}}.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 343, 355. Kami Shrine's events include the {{ill|Mitō-shinji|ja|御頭神事}} on January 15 and its annual festival on July 15. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Ujiyamada City Hall (1929). *Ujiyamada City History*. Vols. 1–2. * Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990). *Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku: Vol. 6, Tōkaidō 1*. Kōgakkan University Press. * Ise Bunka Sha (2008). *O-Ise-san 125 Shrine Pilgrimage*. Ise Bunka Sha. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0605.html Shitomi Shrine] - Jingu Kaikan. * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0606.html Ōkōchi Shrine] - Jingu Kaikan. * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0604.html Uchikake Shrine] - Jingu Kaikan. revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Shitomi Shrine & Ōkōchi Shrine |image = [[File:Shitomi & Okouchi Shrine.jpg|250px]] |location = {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, Tsujikuru 1-chome 13 |Latitude = 34 |Latitude minutes = 29 |Latitude seconds = 23.1 |Longitude = 136 |Longitude minutes = 41 |Longitude seconds = 22.1 |Map = Japan Mie |deity = Shitomi Shrine: {{ill|Kukunochi|ja|久久能智神}}
Ōkōchi Shrine: {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祗神}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (Minor)
{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} Auxiliary Shrine (Sessha) |Founded = Before {{ill|Enryaku era|ja|延暦}} 23 (804 CE) |Main hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} |Alternate names = |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October 19) |Rituals = {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}}, {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭}}, and others }} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[ja:志等美神社]] [[ja:大河内神社]] [[ja:上社]] '''Shitomi Shrine''' (志等美神社) is a shrine in Japan associated with the {{ill|Outer Shrine|ja|外宮}} of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. This article also covers the {{ill|Ōkōchi Shrine|ja|大河内神社}}, which shares the same grounds as Shitomi Shrine, as well as the {{ill|Uchikake Shrine|ja|打懸神社}} and the former village shrine known as {{ill|Kami Shrine (Ise)|ja|上社}}, which encompasses all three shrines within its precincts. Among the 125 shrines affiliated with Ise Grand Shrine, only Shitomi, Ōkōchi, and Uchikake shrines hold recognized ranks in the {{ill|Shinkai (divine rank)|ja|神階|Shinkai (divine rank)=Shinkai (divine rank)|lt=Shinto shrine ranking system}}.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 342, 355. == Overview == Shitomi Shrine is located at Tsuji Kuru 1-13, Ise City, Mie Prefecture. The precincts are under the jurisdiction of Kami Shrine, with Shitomi, Ōkōchi, and Uchikake shrines positioned at the right side of Kami Shrine's eastern entrance.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 949. The total area of the precincts is approximately 700 tsubo (about 2,314 square meters), of which 406 square meters are occupied by Shitomi, Ōkōchi, and Uchikake shrines.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 343, 356. Shitomi and Ōkōchi shrines share the same fence, facing south, and feature {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} architecture with wooden plank roofs. These shrines do not have offering boxes. == Shitomi Shrine == Shitomi Shrine ranks 7th among the 16 auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 1, pp. 7, 11–12. Historical records also refer to it as "Shitomi-no-Iba Shrine," with "Shitomi-no-Iba" being the name of the surrounding wilderness.Ise Bunka Sha (2008): p. 53. Some scholars interpret "Shitomi" as derived from the verb "shitomu," meaning "to soak in water" or "to get wet." Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 336. == Ōkōchi Shrine == Although its structure is separate, Ōkōchi Shrine is located within the same fence as Shitomi Shrine.Ise Bunka Sha (2008): p. 54. It ranks 8th among the 16 auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代}}, Ōkōchi Shrine fell into disrepair and later became a component of Kami Shrine as the "Mountain Shrine."Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 950. The current pronunciation of the shrine's name is "Ōkōchi," but historical documents record readings such as "Ōkahachi" and "Ōgafuchi."Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 354. == Uchikake Shrine == Uchikake Shrine ranks 4th among the eight subsidiary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 1, pp. 13–15. After falling into disrepair during the Sengoku period, it was absorbed into Kami Shrine as "Parallel Shrine" (formerly "Hachioji Shrine"), maintaining its original deity.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 948. == Kami Shrine == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Kami Shrine (上社) |image = [[File:Kami no Shrine, Ise.jpg|250px]] |location = {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, Tsujikuru 1-chome 13 |coordinates = {{coord|34|29|22.3|N|136|41|21.0|E}} |Map = {{location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=29|lat_sec=22.3|lon_deg=136|lon_min=41|lon_sec=21.0|label=Kami Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} |deity = {{ill|Kukunochi|ja|久久能智神}}, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祗神}}, {{ill|Haniyasu|ja|埴安神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂神}}, and others |Rank = Former Village Shrine (旧村社) |Founded = After the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代}} |Main hall = |Alternate names = |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = July 15 |Rituals = {{ill|Onzu-shinji (Head Ritual)|ja|御頭神事}} (January 15) }} Kami Shrine is the local {{ill|ujigami|ja|氏神}}, serving the districts of Nakajima, Tsuji Kuru, Futamata, and Uraguchi. Its treasures include a lion head for {{ill|shishimai|ja|獅子舞}} performances and a sword donated in 1721.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 955. The precincts feature additional shrines such as Inari Shrine, Uegami Shrine dedicated to Sugawara no Michizane, and Kushitama Shrine enshrining Isetsuhiko and Isetsuhime deities, as well as remote worship sites for other shrines.Ise-Shima Kirari 1000 (2013). === Merged Shrines === Kami Shrine absorbed 13 other shrines in 1909.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, pp. 949. == Worship and Festivals == Shitomi Shrine conducts annual festivals, including the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}}, {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭}}, {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|ja|神嘗祭}}, and {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}}.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 343, 355. Kami Shrine's events include the {{ill|Mitō-shinji|ja|御頭神事}} on January 15 and its annual festival on July 15. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Ujiyamada City Hall (1929). *Ujiyamada City History*. Vols. 1–2. * Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990). *Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku: Vol. 6, Tōkaidō 1*. Kōgakkan University Press. * Ise Bunka Sha (2008). *O-Ise-san 125 Shrine Pilgrimage*. Ise Bunka Sha. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0605.html Shitomi Shrine] - Jingu Kaikan. * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0606.html Ōkōchi Shrine] - Jingu Kaikan. * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0604.html Uchikake Shrine] - Jingu Kaikan. {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Shitomi Shrine & Ōkōchi Shrine |image = [[File:Shitomi & Okouchi Shrine.jpg|250px]] |location = {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, Tsujikuru 1-chome 13 |Latitude = 34 |Latitude minutes = 29 |Latitude seconds = 23.1 |Longitude = 136 |Longitude minutes = 41 |Longitude seconds = 22.1 |Map = Japan Mie |deity = Shitomi Shrine: {{ill|Kukunochi|ja|久久能智神}}
Ōkōchi Shrine: {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祗神}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (Minor)
{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} Auxiliary Shrine (Sessha) |Founded = Before {{ill|Enryaku era|ja|延暦}} 23 (804 CE) |Main hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} |Alternate names = |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October 19) |Rituals = {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}}, {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭}}, and others }} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[ja:志等美神社]] [[ja:大河内神社]] [[ja:上社]] '''Shitomi Shrine''' (志等美神社) is a shrine in Japan associated with the {{ill|Outer Shrine|ja|外宮}} of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. This article also covers the {{ill|Ōkōchi Shrine|ja|大河内神社}}, which shares the same grounds as Shitomi Shrine, as well as the {{ill|Uchikake Shrine|ja|打懸神社}} and the former village shrine known as {{ill|Kami Shrine (Ise)|ja|上社}}, which encompasses all three shrines within its precincts. Among the 125 shrines affiliated with Ise Grand Shrine, only Shitomi, Ōkōchi, and Uchikake shrines hold recognized ranks in the {{ill|Shinkai (divine rank)|ja|神階|Shinkai (divine rank)=Shinkai (divine rank)|lt=Shinto shrine ranking system}}.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 342, 355. == Overview == Shitomi Shrine is located at Tsuji Kuru 1-13, Ise City, Mie Prefecture. The precincts are under the jurisdiction of Kami Shrine, with Shitomi, Ōkōchi, and Uchikake shrines positioned at the right side of Kami Shrine's eastern entrance.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 949. The total area of the precincts is approximately 700 tsubo (about 2,314 square meters), of which 406 square meters are occupied by Shitomi, Ōkōchi, and Uchikake shrines.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 343, 356. Shitomi and Ōkōchi shrines share the same fence, facing south, and feature {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} architecture with wooden plank roofs. These shrines do not have offering boxes. == Shitomi Shrine == Shitomi Shrine ranks 7th among the 16 auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 1, pp. 7, 11–12. Historical records also refer to it as "Shitomi-no-Iba Shrine," with "Shitomi-no-Iba" being the name of the surrounding wilderness.Ise Bunka Sha (2008): p. 53. Some scholars interpret "Shitomi" as derived from the verb "shitomu," meaning "to soak in water" or "to get wet." Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 336. == Ōkōchi Shrine == Although its structure is separate, Ōkōchi Shrine is located within the same fence as Shitomi Shrine.Ise Bunka Sha (2008): p. 54. It ranks 8th among the 16 auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代}}, Ōkōchi Shrine fell into disrepair and later became a component of Kami Shrine as the "Mountain Shrine."Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 950. The current pronunciation of the shrine's name is "Ōkōchi," but historical documents record readings such as "Ōkahachi" and "Ōgafuchi."Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 354. == Uchikake Shrine == Uchikake Shrine ranks 4th among the eight subsidiary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 1, pp. 13–15. After falling into disrepair during the Sengoku period, it was absorbed into Kami Shrine as "Parallel Shrine" (formerly "Hachioji Shrine"), maintaining its original deity.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 948. == Kami Shrine == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Kami Shrine (上社) |image = [[File:Kami no Shrine, Ise.jpg|250px]] |location = {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, Tsujikuru 1-chome 13 |coordinates = {{coord|34|29|22.3|N|136|41|21.0|E}} |Map = {{location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=29|lat_sec=22.3|lon_deg=136|lon_min=41|lon_sec=21.0|label=Kami Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} |deity = {{ill|Kukunochi|ja|久久能智神}}, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祗神}}, {{ill|Haniyasu|ja|埴安神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂神}}, and others |Rank = Former Village Shrine (旧村社) |Founded = After the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代}} |Main hall = |Alternate names = |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = July 15 |Rituals = {{ill|Onzu-shinji (Head Ritual)|ja|御頭神事}} (January 15) }} Kami Shrine is the local {{ill|ujigami|ja|氏神}}, serving the districts of Nakajima, Tsuji Kuru, Futamata, and Uraguchi. Its treasures include a lion head for {{ill|shishimai|ja|獅子舞}} performances and a sword donated in 1721.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 955. The precincts feature additional shrines such as Inari Shrine, Uegami Shrine dedicated to Sugawara no Michizane, and Kushitama Shrine enshrining Isetsuhiko and Isetsuhime deities, as well as remote worship sites for other shrines.Ise-Shima Kirari 1000 (2013). === Merged Shrines === Kami Shrine absorbed 13 other shrines in 1909.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, pp. 949. == Worship and Festivals == Shitomi Shrine conducts annual festivals, including the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}}, {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭}}, {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|ja|神嘗祭}}, and {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}}.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 343, 355. Kami Shrine's events include the {{ill|Mitō-shinji|ja|御頭神事}} on January 15 and its annual festival on July 15. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Ujiyamada City Hall (1929). *Ujiyamada City History*. Vols. 1–2. * Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990). *Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku: Vol. 6, Tōkaidō 1*. Kōgakkan University Press. * Ise Bunka Sha (2008). *O-Ise-san 125 Shrine Pilgrimage*. Ise Bunka Sha. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0605.html Shitomi Shrine] - Jingu Kaikan. * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0606.html Ōkōchi Shrine] - Jingu Kaikan. * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0604.html Uchikake Shrine] - Jingu Kaikan. {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Shitomi Shrine & Ōkōchi Shrine |image = [[File:Shitomi & Okouchi Shrine.jpg|250px]] |location = {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, Tsujikuru 1-chome 13 |Latitude = 34 |Latitude minutes = 29 |Latitude seconds = 23.1 |Longitude = 136 |Longitude minutes = 41 |Longitude seconds = 22.1 |Map = Japan Mie |deity = Shitomi Shrine: {{ill|Kukunochi|ja|久久能智神}}
Ōkōchi Shrine: {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祗神}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (Minor)
{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} Auxiliary Shrine (Sessha) |Founded = Before {{ill|Enryaku era|ja|延暦}} 23 (804 CE) |Main hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} |Alternate names = |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October 19) |Rituals = {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}}, {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭}}, and others }} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[ja:志等美神社]] [[ja:大河内神社]] [[ja:上社]] '''Shitomi Shrine''' (志等美神社) is a shrine in Japan associated with the {{ill|Outer Shrine|ja|外宮}} of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. This article also covers the {{ill|Ōkōchi Shrine|ja|大河内神社}}, which shares the same grounds as Shitomi Shrine, as well as the {{ill|Uchikake Shrine|ja|打懸神社}} and the former village shrine known as {{ill|Kami Shrine (Ise)|ja|上社}}, which encompasses all three shrines within its precincts. Among the 125 shrines affiliated with Ise Grand Shrine, only Shitomi, Ōkōchi, and Uchikake shrines hold recognized ranks in the {{ill|Shinkai (divine rank)|ja|神階|Shinkai (divine rank)=Shinkai (divine rank)|lt=Shinto shrine ranking system}}.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 342, 355. == Overview == Shitomi Shrine is located at Tsuji Kuru 1-13, Ise City, Mie Prefecture. The precincts are under the jurisdiction of Kami Shrine, with Shitomi, Ōkōchi, and Uchikake shrines positioned at the right side of Kami Shrine's eastern entrance.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 949. The total area of the precincts is approximately 700 tsubo (about 2,314 square meters), of which 406 square meters are occupied by Shitomi, Ōkōchi, and Uchikake shrines.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 343, 356. Shitomi and Ōkōchi shrines share the same fence, facing south, and feature {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} architecture with wooden plank roofs. These shrines do not have offering boxes. == Shitomi Shrine == Shitomi Shrine ranks 7th among the 16 auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 1, pp. 7, 11–12. Historical records also refer to it as "Shitomi-no-Iba Shrine," with "Shitomi-no-Iba" being the name of the surrounding wilderness.Ise Bunka Sha (2008): p. 53. Some scholars interpret "Shitomi" as derived from the verb "shitomu," meaning "to soak in water" or "to get wet." Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 336. == Ōkōchi Shrine == Although its structure is separate, Ōkōchi Shrine is located within the same fence as Shitomi Shrine.Ise Bunka Sha (2008): p. 54. It ranks 8th among the 16 auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代}}, Ōkōchi Shrine fell into disrepair and later became a component of Kami Shrine as the "Mountain Shrine."Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 950. The current pronunciation of the shrine's name is "Ōkōchi," but historical documents record readings such as "Ōkahachi" and "Ōgafuchi."Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 354. == Uchikake Shrine == Uchikake Shrine ranks 4th among the eight subsidiary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 1, pp. 13–15. After falling into disrepair during the Sengoku period, it was absorbed into Kami Shrine as "Parallel Shrine" (formerly "Hachioji Shrine"), maintaining its original deity.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 948. == Kami Shrine == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Kami Shrine (上社) |image = [[File:Kami no Shrine, Ise.jpg|250px]] |location = {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, Tsujikuru 1-chome 13 |coordinates = {{coord|34|29|22.3|N|136|41|21.0|E}} |Map = {{location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=29|lat_sec=22.3|lon_deg=136|lon_min=41|lon_sec=21.0|label=Kami Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} |deity = {{ill|Kukunochi|ja|久久能智神}}, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祗神}}, {{ill|Haniyasu|ja|埴安神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂神}}, and others |Rank = Former Village Shrine (旧村社) |Founded = After the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代}} |Main hall = |Alternate names = |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = July 15 |Rituals = {{ill|Onzu-shinji (Head Ritual)|ja|御頭神事}} (January 15) }} Kami Shrine is the local {{ill|ujigami|ja|氏神}}, serving the districts of Nakajima, Tsuji Kuru, Futamata, and Uraguchi. Its treasures include a lion head for {{ill|shishimai|ja|獅子舞}} performances and a sword donated in 1721.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 955. The precincts feature additional shrines such as Inari Shrine, Uegami Shrine dedicated to Sugawara no Michizane, and Kushitama Shrine enshrining Isetsuhiko and Isetsuhime deities, as well as remote worship sites for other shrines.Ise-Shima Kirari 1000 (2013). === Merged Shrines === Kami Shrine absorbed 13 other shrines in 1909.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, pp. 949. == Worship and Festivals == Shitomi Shrine conducts annual festivals, including the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}}, {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭}}, {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|ja|神嘗祭}}, and {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}}.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 343, 355. Kami Shrine's events include the {{ill|Mitō-shinji|ja|御頭神事}} on January 15 and its annual festival on July 15. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Ujiyamada City Hall (1929). *Ujiyamada City History*. Vols. 1–2. * Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990). *Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku: Vol. 6, Tōkaidō 1*. Kōgakkan University Press. * Ise Bunka Sha (2008). *O-Ise-san 125 Shrine Pilgrimage*. Ise Bunka Sha. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0605.html Shitomi Shrine] - Jingu Kaikan. * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0606.html Ōkōchi Shrine] - Jingu Kaikan. * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0604.html Uchikake Shrine] - Jingu Kaikan. After falling into disrepair during the Sengoku period, it was absorbed into Kami Shrine as "Parallel Shrine" (formerly "Hachioji Shrine"), maintaining its original deity.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 948. == Kami Shrine == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Kami Shrine (上社) |image = [[File:Kami no Shrine, Ise.jpg|250px]] |location = {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, Tsujikuru 1-chome 13 |coordinates = {{coord|34|29|22.3|N|136|41|21.0|E}} |Map = {{location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=29|lat_sec=22.3|lon_deg=136|lon_min=41|lon_sec=21.0|label=Kami Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} |deity = {{ill|Kukunochi|ja|久久能智神}}, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祗神}}, {{ill|Haniyasu|ja|埴安神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂神}}, and others |Rank = Former Village Shrine (旧村社) |Founded = After the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代}} |Main hall = |Alternate names = |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = July 15 |Rituals = {{ill|Onzu-shinji (Head Ritual)|ja|御頭神事}} (January 15) }} Kami Shrine is the local {{ill|ujigami|ja|氏神}}, serving the districts of Nakajima, Tsuji Kuru, Futamata, and Uraguchi. Its treasures include a lion head for {{ill|shishimai|ja|獅子舞}} performances and a sword donated in 1721.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 955. The precincts feature additional shrines such as Inari Shrine, Uegami Shrine dedicated to Sugawara no Michizane, and Kushitama Shrine enshrining Isetsuhiko and Isetsuhime deities, as well as remote worship sites for other shrines.Ise-Shima Kirari 1000 (2013). === Merged Shrines === Kami Shrine absorbed 13 other shrines in 1909.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, pp. 949. == Worship and Festivals == Shitomi Shrine conducts annual festivals, including the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}}, {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭}}, {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|ja|神嘗祭}}, and {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}}.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 343, 355. Kami Shrine's events include the {{ill|Mitō-shinji|ja|御頭神事}} on January 15 and its annual festival on July 15. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Ujiyamada City Hall (1929). *Ujiyamada City History*. {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Shitomi Shrine & Ōkōchi Shrine |image = [[File:Shitomi & Okouchi Shrine.jpg|250px]] |location = {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, Tsujikuru 1-chome 13 |Latitude = 34 |Latitude minutes = 29 |Latitude seconds = 23.1 |Longitude = 136 |Longitude minutes = 41 |Longitude seconds = 22.1 |Map = Japan Mie |deity = Shitomi Shrine: {{ill|Kukunochi|ja|久久能智神}}
Ōkōchi Shrine: {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祗神}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (Minor)
{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} Auxiliary Shrine (Sessha) |Founded = Before {{ill|Enryaku era|ja|延暦}} 23 (804 CE) |Main hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} |Alternate names = |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October 19) |Rituals = {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}}, {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭}}, and others }} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[ja:志等美神社]] [[ja:大河内神社]] [[ja:上社]] '''Shitomi Shrine''' (志等美神社) is a shrine in Japan associated with the {{ill|Outer Shrine|ja|外宮}} of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. This article also covers the {{ill|Ōkōchi Shrine|ja|大河内神社}}, which shares the same grounds as Shitomi Shrine, as well as the {{ill|Uchikake Shrine|ja|打懸神社}} and the former village shrine known as {{ill|Kami Shrine (Ise)|ja|上社}}, which encompasses all three shrines within its precincts. Among the 125 shrines affiliated with Ise Grand Shrine, only Shitomi, Ōkōchi, and Uchikake shrines hold recognized ranks in the {{ill|Shinkai (divine rank)|ja|神階|Shinkai (divine rank)=Shinkai (divine rank)|lt=Shinto shrine ranking system}}.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 342, 355. == Overview == Shitomi Shrine is located at Tsuji Kuru 1-13, Ise City, Mie Prefecture. The precincts are under the jurisdiction of Kami Shrine, with Shitomi, Ōkōchi, and Uchikake shrines positioned at the right side of Kami Shrine's eastern entrance.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 949. The total area of the precincts is approximately 700 tsubo (about 2,314 square meters), of which 406 square meters are occupied by Shitomi, Ōkōchi, and Uchikake shrines.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 343, 356. Shitomi and Ōkōchi shrines share the same fence, facing south, and feature {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} architecture with wooden plank roofs. These shrines do not have offering boxes. == Shitomi Shrine == Shitomi Shrine ranks 7th among the 16 auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 1, pp. 7, 11–12. Historical records also refer to it as "Shitomi-no-Iba Shrine," with "Shitomi-no-Iba" being the name of the surrounding wilderness.Ise Bunka Sha (2008): p. 53. Some scholars interpret "Shitomi" as derived from the verb "shitomu," meaning "to soak in water" or "to get wet." Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 336. == Ōkōchi Shrine == Although its structure is separate, Ōkōchi Shrine is located within the same fence as Shitomi Shrine.Ise Bunka Sha (2008): p. 54. It ranks 8th among the 16 auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代}}, Ōkōchi Shrine fell into disrepair and later became a component of Kami Shrine as the "Mountain Shrine."Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 950. The current pronunciation of the shrine's name is "Ōkōchi," but historical documents record readings such as "Ōkahachi" and "Ōgafuchi."Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 354. == Uchikake Shrine == Uchikake Shrine ranks 4th among the eight subsidiary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 1, pp. 13–15. After falling into disrepair during the Sengoku period, it was absorbed into Kami Shrine as "Parallel Shrine" (formerly "Hachioji Shrine"), maintaining its original deity.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 948. == Kami Shrine == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Kami Shrine (上社) |image = [[File:Kami no Shrine, Ise.jpg|250px]] |location = {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, Tsujikuru 1-chome 13 |coordinates = {{coord|34|29|22.3|N|136|41|21.0|E}} |Map = {{location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=29|lat_sec=22.3|lon_deg=136|lon_min=41|lon_sec=21.0|label=Kami Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} |deity = {{ill|Kukunochi|ja|久久能智神}}, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祗神}}, {{ill|Haniyasu|ja|埴安神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂神}}, and others |Rank = Former Village Shrine (旧村社) |Founded = After the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代}} |Main hall = |Alternate names = |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = July 15 |Rituals = {{ill|Onzu-shinji (Head Ritual)|ja|御頭神事}} (January 15) }} Kami Shrine is the local {{ill|ujigami|ja|氏神}}, serving the districts of Nakajima, Tsuji Kuru, Futamata, and Uraguchi. Its treasures include a lion head for {{ill|shishimai|ja|獅子舞}} performances and a sword donated in 1721.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 955. The precincts feature additional shrines such as Inari Shrine, Uegami Shrine dedicated to Sugawara no Michizane, and Kushitama Shrine enshrining Isetsuhiko and Isetsuhime deities, as well as remote worship sites for other shrines.Ise-Shima Kirari 1000 (2013). === Merged Shrines === Kami Shrine absorbed 13 other shrines in 1909.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, pp. 949. == Worship and Festivals == Shitomi Shrine conducts annual festivals, including the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}}, {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭}}, {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|ja|神嘗祭}}, and {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}}.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 343, 355. Kami Shrine's events include the {{ill|Mitō-shinji|ja|御頭神事}} on January 15 and its annual festival on July 15. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Ujiyamada City Hall (1929). *Ujiyamada City History*. Vols. 1–2. * Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990). *Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku: Vol. 6, Tōkaidō 1*. Kōgakkan University Press. * Ise Bunka Sha (2008). *O-Ise-san 125 Shrine Pilgrimage*. The current pronunciation of the shrine's name is "Ōkōchi," but historical documents record readings such as "Ōkahachi" and "Ōgafuchi."Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 354. == Uchikake Shrine == Uchikake Shrine ranks 4th among the eight subsidiary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 1, pp. 13–15. After falling into disrepair during the Sengoku period, it was absorbed into Kami Shrine as "Parallel Shrine" (formerly "Hachioji Shrine"), maintaining its original deity.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 948. == Kami Shrine == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Kami Shrine (上社) |image = [[File:Kami no Shrine, Ise.jpg|250px]] |location = {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, Tsujikuru 1-chome 13 |coordinates = {{coord|34|29|22.3|N|136|41|21.0|E}} |Map = {{location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=29|lat_sec=22.3|lon_deg=136|lon_min=41|lon_sec=21.0|label=Kami Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} |deity = {{ill|Kukunochi|ja|久久能智神}}, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祗神}}, {{ill|Haniyasu|ja|埴安神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂神}}, and others |Rank = Former Village Shrine (旧村社) |Founded = After the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代}} |Main hall = |Alternate names = |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = July 15 |Rituals = {{ill|Onzu-shinji (Head Ritual)|ja|御頭神事}} (January 15) }} Kami Shrine is the local {{ill|ujigami|ja|氏神}}, serving the districts of Nakajima, Tsuji Kuru, Futamata, and Uraguchi. Its treasures include a lion head for {{ill|shishimai|ja|獅子舞}} performances and a sword donated in 1721.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 955. The precincts feature additional shrines such as Inari Shrine, Uegami Shrine dedicated to Sugawara no Michizane, and Kushitama Shrine enshrining Isetsuhiko and Isetsuhime deities, as well as remote worship sites for other shrines.Ise-Shima Kirari 1000 (2013). === Merged Shrines === Kami Shrine absorbed 13 other shrines in 1909.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, pp. 949. == Worship and Festivals == Shitomi Shrine conducts annual festivals, including the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}}, {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭}}, {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|ja|神嘗祭}}, and {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}}.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 343, 355. Kami Shrine's events include the {{ill|Mitō-shinji|ja|御頭神事}} on January 15 and its annual festival on July 15. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Ujiyamada City Hall (1929). *Ujiyamada City History*. Vols. 1–2. * Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990). *Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku: Vol. 6, Tōkaidō 1*. Kōgakkan University Press. * Ise Bunka Sha (2008). *O-Ise-san 125 Shrine Pilgrimage*. {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Shitomi Shrine & Ōkōchi Shrine |image = [[File:Shitomi & Okouchi Shrine.jpg|250px]] |location = {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, Tsujikuru 1-chome 13 |Latitude = 34 |Latitude minutes = 29 |Latitude seconds = 23.1 |Longitude = 136 |Longitude minutes = 41 |Longitude seconds = 22.1 |Map = Japan Mie |deity = Shitomi Shrine: {{ill|Kukunochi|ja|久久能智神}}
Ōkōchi Shrine: {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祗神}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (Minor)
{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} Auxiliary Shrine (Sessha) |Founded = Before {{ill|Enryaku era|ja|延暦}} 23 (804 CE) |Main hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} |Alternate names = |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October 19) |Rituals = {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}}, {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭}}, and others }} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[ja:志等美神社]] [[ja:大河内神社]] [[ja:上社]] '''Shitomi Shrine''' (志等美神社) is a shrine in Japan associated with the {{ill|Outer Shrine|ja|外宮}} of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. This article also covers the {{ill|Ōkōchi Shrine|ja|大河内神社}}, which shares the same grounds as Shitomi Shrine, as well as the {{ill|Uchikake Shrine|ja|打懸神社}} and the former village shrine known as {{ill|Kami Shrine (Ise)|ja|上社}}, which encompasses all three shrines within its precincts. Among the 125 shrines affiliated with Ise Grand Shrine, only Shitomi, Ōkōchi, and Uchikake shrines hold recognized ranks in the {{ill|Shinkai (divine rank)|ja|神階|Shinkai (divine rank)=Shinkai (divine rank)|lt=Shinto shrine ranking system}}.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 342, 355. == Overview == Shitomi Shrine is located at Tsuji Kuru 1-13, Ise City, Mie Prefecture. The precincts are under the jurisdiction of Kami Shrine, with Shitomi, Ōkōchi, and Uchikake shrines positioned at the right side of Kami Shrine's eastern entrance.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 949. The total area of the precincts is approximately 700 tsubo (about 2,314 square meters), of which 406 square meters are occupied by Shitomi, Ōkōchi, and Uchikake shrines.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 343, 356. Shitomi and Ōkōchi shrines share the same fence, facing south, and feature {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} architecture with wooden plank roofs. These shrines do not have offering boxes. == Shitomi Shrine == Shitomi Shrine ranks 7th among the 16 auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 1, pp. 7, 11–12. Historical records also refer to it as "Shitomi-no-Iba Shrine," with "Shitomi-no-Iba" being the name of the surrounding wilderness.Ise Bunka Sha (2008): p. 53. Some scholars interpret "Shitomi" as derived from the verb "shitomu," meaning "to soak in water" or "to get wet." Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 336. == Ōkōchi Shrine == Although its structure is separate, Ōkōchi Shrine is located within the same fence as Shitomi Shrine.Ise Bunka Sha (2008): p. 54. It ranks 8th among the 16 auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代}}, Ōkōchi Shrine fell into disrepair and later became a component of Kami Shrine as the "Mountain Shrine."Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 950. The current pronunciation of the shrine's name is "Ōkōchi," but historical documents record readings such as "Ōkahachi" and "Ōgafuchi."Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 354. == Uchikake Shrine == Uchikake Shrine ranks 4th among the eight subsidiary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 1, pp. 13–15. After falling into disrepair during the Sengoku period, it was absorbed into Kami Shrine as "Parallel Shrine" (formerly "Hachioji Shrine"), maintaining its original deity.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 948. == Kami Shrine == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Kami Shrine (上社) |image = [[File:Kami no Shrine, Ise.jpg|250px]] |location = {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, Tsujikuru 1-chome 13 |coordinates = {{coord|34|29|22.3|N|136|41|21.0|E}} |Map = {{location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=29|lat_sec=22.3|lon_deg=136|lon_min=41|lon_sec=21.0|label=Kami Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} |deity = {{ill|Kukunochi|ja|久久能智神}}, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祗神}}, {{ill|Haniyasu|ja|埴安神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂神}}, and others |Rank = Former Village Shrine (旧村社) |Founded = After the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代}} |Main hall = |Alternate names = |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = July 15 |Rituals = {{ill|Onzu-shinji (Head Ritual)|ja|御頭神事}} (January 15) }} Kami Shrine is the local {{ill|ujigami|ja|氏神}}, serving the districts of Nakajima, Tsuji Kuru, Futamata, and Uraguchi. Its treasures include a lion head for {{ill|shishimai|ja|獅子舞}} performances and a sword donated in 1721.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 955. The precincts feature additional shrines such as Inari Shrine, Uegami Shrine dedicated to Sugawara no Michizane, and Kushitama Shrine enshrining Isetsuhiko and Isetsuhime deities, as well as remote worship sites for other shrines.Ise-Shima Kirari 1000 (2013). === Merged Shrines === Kami Shrine absorbed 13 other shrines in 1909.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, pp. 949. == Worship and Festivals == Shitomi Shrine conducts annual festivals, including the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}}, {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭}}, {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|ja|神嘗祭}}, and {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}}.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 343, 355. Kami Shrine's events include the {{ill|Mitō-shinji|ja|御頭神事}} on January 15 and its annual festival on July 15. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Ujiyamada City Hall (1929). *Ujiyamada City History*. Vols. 1–2. * Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990). *Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku: Vol. 6, Tōkaidō 1*. Kōgakkan University Press. * Ise Bunka Sha (2008). *O-Ise-san 125 Shrine Pilgrimage*. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代}}, Ōkōchi Shrine fell into disrepair and later became a component of Kami Shrine as the "Mountain Shrine."Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 950. The current pronunciation of the shrine's name is "Ōkōchi," but historical documents record readings such as "Ōkahachi" and "Ōgafuchi."Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 354. == Uchikake Shrine == Uchikake Shrine ranks 4th among the eight subsidiary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 1, pp. 13–15. After falling into disrepair during the Sengoku period, it was absorbed into Kami Shrine as "Parallel Shrine" (formerly "Hachioji Shrine"), maintaining its original deity.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 948. == Kami Shrine == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Kami Shrine (上社) |image = [[File:Kami no Shrine, Ise.jpg|250px]] |location = {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, Tsujikuru 1-chome 13 |coordinates = {{coord|34|29|22.3|N|136|41|21.0|E}} |Map = {{location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=29|lat_sec=22.3|lon_deg=136|lon_min=41|lon_sec=21.0|label=Kami Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} |deity = {{ill|Kukunochi|ja|久久能智神}}, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祗神}}, {{ill|Haniyasu|ja|埴安神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂神}}, and others |Rank = Former Village Shrine (旧村社) |Founded = After the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代}} |Main hall = |Alternate names = |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = July 15 |Rituals = {{ill|Onzu-shinji (Head Ritual)|ja|御頭神事}} (January 15) }} Kami Shrine is the local {{ill|ujigami|ja|氏神}}, serving the districts of Nakajima, Tsuji Kuru, Futamata, and Uraguchi. Its treasures include a lion head for {{ill|shishimai|ja|獅子舞}} performances and a sword donated in 1721.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 955. The precincts feature additional shrines such as Inari Shrine, Uegami Shrine dedicated to Sugawara no Michizane, and Kushitama Shrine enshrining Isetsuhiko and Isetsuhime deities, as well as remote worship sites for other shrines.Ise-Shima Kirari 1000 (2013). === Merged Shrines === Kami Shrine absorbed 13 other shrines in 1909.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, pp. 949. == Worship and Festivals == Shitomi Shrine conducts annual festivals, including the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}}, {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭}}, {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|ja|神嘗祭}}, and {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}}.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 343, 355. Kami Shrine's events include the {{ill|Mitō-shinji|ja|御頭神事}} on January 15 and its annual festival on July 15. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Ujiyamada City Hall (1929). *Ujiyamada City History*. Vols. 1–2. * Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990). *Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku: Vol. 6, Tōkaidō 1*. Kōgakkan University Press. * Ise Bunka Sha (2008). *O-Ise-san 125 Shrine Pilgrimage*. Ise Bunka Sha. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0605.html Shitomi Shrine] - Jingu Kaikan. * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0606.html Ōkōchi Shrine] - Jingu Kaikan. * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0604.html Uchikake Shrine] - Jingu Kaikan. {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Shitomi Shrine & Ōkōchi Shrine |image = [[File:Shitomi & Okouchi Shrine.jpg|250px]] |location = {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, Tsujikuru 1-chome 13 |Latitude = 34 |Latitude minutes = 29 |Latitude seconds = 23.1 |Longitude = 136 |Longitude minutes = 41 |Longitude seconds = 22.1 |Map = Japan Mie |deity = Shitomi Shrine: {{ill|Kukunochi|ja|久久能智神}}
Ōkōchi Shrine: {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祗神}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (Minor)
{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} Auxiliary Shrine (Sessha) |Founded = Before {{ill|Enryaku era|ja|延暦}} 23 (804 CE) |Main hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} |Alternate names = |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October 19) |Rituals = {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}}, {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭}}, and others }} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[ja:志等美神社]] [[ja:大河内神社]] [[ja:上社]] '''Shitomi Shrine''' (志等美神社) is a shrine in Japan associated with the {{ill|Outer Shrine|ja|外宮}} of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. This article also covers the {{ill|Ōkōchi Shrine|ja|大河内神社}}, which shares the same grounds as Shitomi Shrine, as well as the {{ill|Uchikake Shrine|ja|打懸神社}} and the former village shrine known as {{ill|Kami Shrine (Ise)|ja|上社}}, which encompasses all three shrines within its precincts. Among the 125 shrines affiliated with Ise Grand Shrine, only Shitomi, Ōkōchi, and Uchikake shrines hold recognized ranks in the {{ill|Shinkai (divine rank)|ja|神階|Shinkai (divine rank)=Shinkai (divine rank)|lt=Shinto shrine ranking system}}.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 342, 355. == Overview == Shitomi Shrine is located at Tsuji Kuru 1-13, Ise City, Mie Prefecture. The precincts are under the jurisdiction of Kami Shrine, with Shitomi, Ōkōchi, and Uchikake shrines positioned at the right side of Kami Shrine's eastern entrance.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 949. The total area of the precincts is approximately 700 tsubo (about 2,314 square meters), of which 406 square meters are occupied by Shitomi, Ōkōchi, and Uchikake shrines.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 343, 356. Shitomi and Ōkōchi shrines share the same fence, facing south, and feature {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} architecture with wooden plank roofs. These shrines do not have offering boxes. == Shitomi Shrine == Shitomi Shrine ranks 7th among the 16 auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 1, pp. 7, 11–12. Historical records also refer to it as "Shitomi-no-Iba Shrine," with "Shitomi-no-Iba" being the name of the surrounding wilderness.Ise Bunka Sha (2008): p. 53. Some scholars interpret "Shitomi" as derived from the verb "shitomu," meaning "to soak in water" or "to get wet." Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 336. == Ōkōchi Shrine == Although its structure is separate, Ōkōchi Shrine is located within the same fence as Shitomi Shrine.Ise Bunka Sha (2008): p. 54. It ranks 8th among the 16 auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代}}, Ōkōchi Shrine fell into disrepair and later became a component of Kami Shrine as the "Mountain Shrine."Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 950. The current pronunciation of the shrine's name is "Ōkōchi," but historical documents record readings such as "Ōkahachi" and "Ōgafuchi."Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 354. == Uchikake Shrine == Uchikake Shrine ranks 4th among the eight subsidiary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 1, pp. 13–15. After falling into disrepair during the Sengoku period, it was absorbed into Kami Shrine as "Parallel Shrine" (formerly "Hachioji Shrine"), maintaining its original deity.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 948. == Kami Shrine == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Kami Shrine (上社) |image = [[File:Kami no Shrine, Ise.jpg|250px]] |location = {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, Tsujikuru 1-chome 13 |coordinates = {{coord|34|29|22.3|N|136|41|21.0|E}} |Map = {{location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=29|lat_sec=22.3|lon_deg=136|lon_min=41|lon_sec=21.0|label=Kami Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} |deity = {{ill|Kukunochi|ja|久久能智神}}, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祗神}}, {{ill|Haniyasu|ja|埴安神}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂神}}, and others |Rank = Former Village Shrine (旧村社) |Founded = After the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代}} |Main hall = |Alternate names = |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = July 15 |Rituals = {{ill|Onzu-shinji (Head Ritual)|ja|御頭神事}} (January 15) }} Kami Shrine is the local {{ill|ujigami|ja|氏神}}, serving the districts of Nakajima, Tsuji Kuru, Futamata, and Uraguchi. Its treasures include a lion head for {{ill|shishimai|ja|獅子舞}} performances and a sword donated in 1721.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 955. The precincts feature additional shrines such as Inari Shrine, Uegami Shrine dedicated to Sugawara no Michizane, and Kushitama Shrine enshrining Isetsuhiko and Isetsuhime deities, as well as remote worship sites for other shrines.Ise-Shima Kirari 1000 (2013). === Merged Shrines === Kami Shrine absorbed 13 other shrines in 1909.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, pp. 949. == Worship and Festivals == Shitomi Shrine conducts annual festivals, including the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}}, {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭}}, {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|ja|神嘗祭}}, and {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}}.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 343, 355. Kami Shrine's events include the {{ill|Mitō-shinji|ja|御頭神事}} on January 15 and its annual festival on July 15. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Ujiyamada City Hall (1929). *Ujiyamada City History*. Vols. 1–2. * Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990). *Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku: Vol. 6, Tōkaidō 1*. Kōgakkan University Press. * Ise Bunka Sha (2008). *O-Ise-san 125 Shrine Pilgrimage*. Ise Bunka Sha. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0605.html Shitomi Shrine] - Jingu Kaikan. * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0606.html Ōkōchi Shrine] - Jingu Kaikan. * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0604.html Uchikake Shrine] - Jingu Kaikan.
