After falling into disrepair during the Sengoku period, it was absorbed into Kami Shrine as "Parallel Shrine" (formerly "Hachioji Shrine"), maintaining its original deity.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 948. == Kami Shrine == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Kami Shrine (上社) |image = [[File:Kami no Shrine, Ise.jpg|250px]] |location = [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], Tsujikuru 1-chome 13 |coordinates = {{coord|34|29|22.3|N|136|41|21.0|E}} |Map = {{location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=29|lat_sec=22.3|lon_deg=136|lon_min=41|lon_sec=21.0|label=Kami Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} |deity = {{ill|Kukunochi|ja|久久能智神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukunochi|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}}, {{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu|ja|埴安神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu|qq=}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂神|1=Ukanomitama|2=ja|3=ウカノミタマ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}}, and others |Rank = Former Village Shrine (旧村社) |Founded = After the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}} |Main hall = |alternate_name = |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = July 15 |Rituals = {{ill|Onzu-shinji (Head Ritual)|ja|御頭神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onzu-shinji (Head Ritual)|qq=}} (January 15) }} Kami Shrine is the local [[:en:Ujigami|ujigami]], serving the districts of Nakajima, Tsuji Kuru, Futamata, and Uraguchi. Its treasures include a lion head for [[:en:Lion dance|shishimai]] performances and a sword donated in 1721.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 955. The precincts feature additional shrines such as Inari Shrine, Uegami Shrine dedicated to Sugawara no Michizane, and Kushitama Shrine enshrining Isetsuhiko and Isetsuhime deities, as well as remote {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship sites|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} for other shrines.Ise-Shima Kirari 1000 (2013). === Merged Shrines === Kami Shrine absorbed 13 other shrines in 1909.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, pp. 949. == Worship and Festivals == Shitomi Shrine conducts annual festivals, including the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]], {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}}, [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai Festival]], and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]].Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 343, 355. Kami Shrine's events include the {{ill|Mitō-shinji|ja|御頭神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitō-shinji|qq=}} on January 15 and its annual festival on July 15. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Ujiyamada City Hall (1929). *Ujiyamada City History*. Vols. 1–2. * Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990). *Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku: Vol. 6, Tōkaidō 1*. Kōgakkan University Press. * Ise Bunka Sha (2008). *O-Ise-san 125 Shrine Pilgrimage*. Ise Bunka Sha. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0605.html Shitomi Shrine] - Jingu Kaikan. * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0606.html Ōkōchi Shrine] - Jingu Kaikan. * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0604.html Uchikake Shrine] - Jingu Kaikan. {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|志等美神社|version=86517017|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Auxiliary Shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] }} [[ja:志等美神社]] [[ja:大河内神社]] [[ja:上社]] [[sn:Shitomi Shrine]] [[sn:hitomi Shrine]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Shitomi Shrine & Ōkōchi Shrine |image = [[File:Shitomi & Okouchi Shrine.jpg|250px]] |location = [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Tsujikuru 1-chome 13 |Latitude = 34 |Latitude minutes = 29 |Latitude seconds = 23.1 |Longitude = 136 |Longitude minutes = 41 |Longitude seconds = 22.1 |Map = Japan Mie |deity = Shitomi Shrine: {{ill|Kukunochi|ja|久久能智神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukunochi|qq=}}
Ōkōchi Shrine: {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (Minor)
{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=}} Auxiliary Shrine (Sessha) |Founded = Before [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku era]] 23 (804 CE) |Main hall = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] |alternate_name = |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (October 19) |Rituals = [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]], {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}}, and others }} '''Shitomi Shrine''' (志等美神社) is a shrine in Japan associated with the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Outer Shrine|ja|外宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Outer Shrine|qq=}} of the [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. This article also covers the {{ill|Shitomi Shrine|lt=Shitomi Shrine|lt=Ōkōchi Shrine|ja|大河内神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkōchi Shrine|qq=}}, which shares the same grounds as Shitomi Shrine, as well as the {{ill|Shitomi Shrine|lt=Shitomi Shrine|lt=Uchikake Shrine|ja|打懸神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchikake Shrine|qq=}} and the former village shrine known as {{ill|Shitomi Shrine|lt=Shitomi Shrine|lt=Kami Shrine (Ise)|ja|上社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}, which encompasses all three shrines within its precincts. Among the 125 shrines affiliated with Ise Grand Shrine, only Shitomi, Ōkōchi, and Uchikake shrines hold recognized Ranks in the [[:en:Shinkai (divine rank)|Shinto shrine Ranking system]].Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 342, 355. == Overview == Shitomi Shrine is located at Tsuji Kuru 1-13, Ise City, Mie Prefecture. The precincts are under the jurisdiction of Kami Shrine, with Shitomi, Ōkōchi, and Uchikake shrines positioned at the right side of Kami Shrine's eastern entrance.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 949. The total area of the precincts is approximately 700 tsubo (about 2,314 square meters), of which 406 square meters are occupied by Shitomi, Ōkōchi, and Uchikake shrines.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 343, 356. Shitomi and Ōkōchi shrines share the same fence, facing south, and feature [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] architecture with wooden plank roofs. These shrines do not have offering boxes. == Shitomi Shrine == Shitomi Shrine Ranks 7th among the 16 auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 1, pp. 7, 11–12. Historical records also refer to it as "Shitomi-no-Iba Shrine," with "Shitomi-no-Iba" being the name of the surrounding wilderness.Ise Bunka Sha (2008): p. 53. Some scholars interpret "Shitomi" as derived from the verb "shitomu," meaning "to soak in water" or "to get wet." Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 336. == Ōkōchi Shrine == Although its structure is separate, Ōkōchi Shrine is located within the same fence as Shitomi Shrine.Ise Bunka Sha (2008): p. 54. It Ranks 8th among the 16 auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}}, Ōkōchi Shrine fell into disrepair and later became a component of Kami Shrine as the "Mountain Shrine."Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 950. The current pronunciation of the shrine's name is "Ōkōchi," but historical documents record readings such as "Ōkahachi" and "Ōgafuchi."Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 354. == Uchikake Shrine == Uchikake Shrine Ranks 4th among the eight subsidiary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 1, pp. 13–15. After falling into disrepair during the Sengoku period, it was absorbed into Kami Shrine as "Parallel Shrine" (formerly "Hachioji Shrine"), maintaining its original deity.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 948. == Kami Shrine == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Kami Shrine (上社) |image = [[File:Kami no Shrine, Ise.jpg|250px]] |location = [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], Tsujikuru 1-chome 13 |coordinates = {{coord|34|29|22.3|N|136|41|21.0|E}} |Map = {{location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=29|lat_sec=22.3|lon_deg=136|lon_min=41|lon_sec=21.0|label=Kami Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} |deity = {{ill|Kukunochi|ja|久久能智神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukunochi|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}}, {{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu|ja|埴安神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu|qq=}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂神|1=Ukanomitama|2=ja|3=ウカノミタマ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}}, and others |Rank = Former Village Shrine (旧村社) |Founded = After the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}} |Main hall = |alternate_name = |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = July 15 |Rituals = {{ill|Onzu-shinji (Head Ritual)|ja|御頭神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onzu-shinji (Head Ritual)|qq=}} (January 15) }} Kami Shrine is the local [[:en:Ujigami|ujigami]], serving the districts of Nakajima, Tsuji Kuru, Futamata, and Uraguchi. Its treasures include a lion head for [[:en:Lion dance|shishimai]] performances and a sword donated in 1721.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 955. The precincts feature additional shrines such as Inari Shrine, Uegami Shrine dedicated to Sugawara no Michizane, and Kushitama Shrine enshrining Isetsuhiko and Isetsuhime deities, as well as remote {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship sites|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} for other shrines.Ise-Shima Kirari 1000 (2013). === Merged Shrines === Kami Shrine absorbed 13 other shrines in 1909.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, pp. 949. == Worship and Festivals == Shitomi Shrine conducts annual festivals, including the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]], {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}}, [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai Festival]], and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]].Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 343, 355. Kami Shrine's events include the {{ill|Mitō-shinji|ja|御頭神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitō-shinji|qq=}} on January 15 and its annual festival on July 15. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Ujiyamada City Hall (1929). *Ujiyamada City History*. Vols. 1–2. * Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990). *Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku: Vol. 6, Tōkaidō 1*. Kōgakkan University Press. * Ise Bunka Sha (2008). *O-Ise-san 125 Shrine Pilgrimage*. Ise Bunka Sha. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0605.html Shitomi Shrine] - Jingu Kaikan. * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0606.html Ōkōchi Shrine] - Jingu Kaikan. * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0604.html Uchikake Shrine] - Jingu Kaikan. {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|志等美神社|version=86517017|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Auxiliary Shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] }} [[ja:志等美神社]] [[ja:大河内神社]] [[ja:上社]] [[sn:Shitomi Shrine]] [[sn:hitomi Shrine]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] {{Commons category|Kaminoyashiro, Ise}}

The precincts feature additional shrines such as Inari Shrine, Uegami Shrine dedicated to Sugawara no Michizane, and Kushitama Shrine enshrining Isetsuhiko and Isetsuhime deities, as well as remote {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship sites|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} for other shrines.Ise-Shima Kirari 1000 (2013). === Merged Shrines === Kami Shrine absorbed 13 other shrines in 1909.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, pp. 949. == Worship and Festivals == Shitomi Shrine conducts annual festivals, including the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]], {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}}, [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai Festival]], and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]].Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 343, 355. Kami Shrine's events include the {{ill|Mitō-shinji|ja|御頭神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitō-shinji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on January 15 and its annual festival on July 15. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Ujiyamada City Hall (1929). *Ujiyamada City History*. Vols. 1–2. * Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990). *Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku: Vol. 6, Tōkaidō 1*. Kōgakkan University Press. * Ise Bunka Sha (2008). *O-Ise-san 125 Shrine Pilgrimage*. Ise Bunka Sha. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0605.html Shitomi Shrine] - Jingu Kaikan. * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0606.html Ōkōchi Shrine] - Jingu Kaikan. * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0604.html Uchikake Shrine] - Jingu Kaikan. {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|志等美神社|version=86517017|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Auxiliary Shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] }} [[ja:志等美神社]] [[ja:大河内神社]] [[ja:上社]] [[sn:Shitomi Shrine]] [[sn:hitomi Shrine]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] {{Commons category|Kaminoyashiro, Ise}}

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Auxiliary Shrines
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Shitomi Shrine & Ōkōchi Shrine |image = [[File:Shitomi & Okouchi Shrine.jpg|250px]] |location = [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Tsujikuru 1-chome 13 |Latitude = 34 |Latitude minutes = 29 |Latitude seconds = 23.1 |Longitude = 136 |Longitude minutes = 41 |Longitude seconds = 22.1 |Map = Japan Mie |deity = Shitomi Shrine: {{ill|Kukunochi|ja|久久能智神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukunochi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ククノチ}}
Ōkōchi Shrine: {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} (Minor)
{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Auxiliary Shrine (Sessha) |Founded = Before [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku era]] 23 (804 CE) |Main hall = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] |alternate_name = |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (October 19) |Rituals = [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]], {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}}, and others }} '''Shitomi Shrine''' (志等美神社) is a shrine in Japan associated with the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Outer Shrine|ja|外宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Outer Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊受大神宮}} of the [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. This article also covers the {{ill|Shitomi Shrine|lt=Shitomi Shrine|lt=Ōkōchi Shrine|ja|大河内神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkōchi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 志等美神社}}, which shares the same grounds as Shitomi Shrine, as well as the {{ill|Shitomi Shrine|lt=Shitomi Shrine|lt=Uchikake Shrine|ja|打懸神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchikake Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 志等美神社}} and the former village shrine known as {{ill|Shitomi Shrine|lt=Shitomi Shrine|lt=Kami Shrine (Ise)|ja|上社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}, which encompasses all three shrines within its precincts. Among the 125 shrines affiliated with Ise Grand Shrine, only Shitomi, Ōkōchi, and Uchikake shrines hold recognized Ranks in the [[:en:Shinkai (divine rank)|Shinto shrine Ranking system]].Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 342, 355. == Overview == Shitomi Shrine is located at Tsuji Kuru 1-13, Ise City, Mie Prefecture. The precincts are under the jurisdiction of Kami Shrine, with Shitomi, Ōkōchi, and Uchikake shrines positioned at the right side of Kami Shrine's eastern entrance.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 949. The total area of the precincts is approximately 700 tsubo (about 2,314 square meters), of which 406 square meters are occupied by Shitomi, Ōkōchi, and Uchikake shrines.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 343, 356. Shitomi and Ōkōchi shrines share the same fence, facing south, and feature [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] architecture with wooden plank roofs. These shrines do not have offering boxes. == Shitomi Shrine == Shitomi Shrine Ranks 7th among the 16 auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 1, pp. 7, 11–12. Historical records also refer to it as "Shitomi-no-Iba Shrine," with "Shitomi-no-Iba" being the name of the surrounding wilderness.Ise Bunka Sha (2008): p. 53. Some scholars interpret "Shitomi" as derived from the verb "shitomu," meaning "to soak in water" or "to get wet." Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 336. == Ōkōchi Shrine == Although its structure is separate, Ōkōchi Shrine is located within the same fence as Shitomi Shrine.Ise Bunka Sha (2008): p. 54. It Ranks 8th among the 16 auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}}, Ōkōchi Shrine fell into disrepair and later became a component of Kami Shrine as the "Mountain Shrine."Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 950. The current pronunciation of the shrine's name is "Ōkōchi," but historical documents record readings such as "Ōkahachi" and "Ōgafuchi."Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 354. == Uchikake Shrine == Uchikake Shrine Ranks 4th among the eight subsidiary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 1, pp. 13–15. After falling into disrepair during the Sengoku period, it was absorbed into Kami Shrine as "Parallel Shrine" (formerly "Hachioji Shrine"), maintaining its original deity.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 948. == Kami Shrine == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Kami Shrine (上社) |image = [[File:Kami no Shrine, Ise.jpg|250px]] |location = [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], Tsujikuru 1-chome 13 |coordinates = {{coord|34|29|22.3|N|136|41|21.0|E}} |Map = {{location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=29|lat_sec=22.3|lon_deg=136|lon_min=41|lon_sec=21.0|label=Kami Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} |deity = {{ill|Kukunochi|ja|久久能智神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukunochi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ククノチ}}, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu|ja|埴安神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ハニヤス}}, [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama]], and others |Rank = Former Village Shrine (旧村社) |Founded = After the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}} |Main hall = |alternate_name = |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = July 15 |Rituals = {{ill|Onzu-shinji (Head Ritual)|ja|御頭神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onzu-shinji (Head Ritual)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (January 15) }} Kami Shrine is the local [[:en:Ujigami|ujigami]], serving the districts of Nakajima, Tsuji Kuru, Futamata, and Uraguchi. Its treasures include a lion head for [[:en:Lion dance|shishimai]] performances and a sword donated in 1721.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 955. The precincts feature additional shrines such as Inari Shrine, Uegami Shrine dedicated to Sugawara no Michizane, and Kushitama Shrine enshrining Isetsuhiko and Isetsuhime deities, as well as remote {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship sites|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} for other shrines.Ise-Shima Kirari 1000 (2013). === Merged Shrines === Kami Shrine absorbed 13 other shrines in 1909.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, pp. 949. == Worship and Festivals == Shitomi Shrine conducts annual festivals, including the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]], {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}}, [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai Festival]], and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]].Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 343, 355. Kami Shrine's events include the {{ill|Mitō-shinji|ja|御頭神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitō-shinji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on January 15 and its annual festival on July 15. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Ujiyamada City Hall (1929). *Ujiyamada City History*. Vols. 1–2. * Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990). *Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku: Vol. 6, Tōkaidō 1*. Kōgakkan University Press. * Ise Bunka Sha (2008). *O-Ise-san 125 Shrine Pilgrimage*. Ise Bunka Sha. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0605.html Shitomi Shrine] - Jingu Kaikan. * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0606.html Ōkōchi Shrine] - Jingu Kaikan. * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0604.html Uchikake Shrine] - Jingu Kaikan. {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|志等美神社|version=86517017|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] }} [[ja:志等美神社]] [[ja:大河内神社]] [[ja:上社]] [[sn:Shitomi Shrine]] [[sn:hitomi Shrine]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] {{Commons category|Kaminoyashiro, Ise}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Shitomi Shrine & Ōkōchi Shrine |image = [[File:Shitomi & Okouchi Shrine.jpg|250px]] |location = [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Tsujikuru 1-chome 13 |Latitude = 34 |Latitude minutes = 29 |Latitude seconds = 23.1 |Longitude = 136 |Longitude minutes = 41 |Longitude seconds = 22.1 |Map = Japan Mie |deity = Shitomi Shrine: {{ill|Kukunochi|ja|久久能智神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukunochi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ククノチ}}
Ōkōchi Shrine: {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor)
{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Auxiliary Shrine (Sessha) |Founded = Before [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku era]] 23 (804 CE) |Main hall = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] |alternate_name = |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (October 19) |Rituals = [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]], {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}}, and others }} '''Shitomi Shrine''' (志等美神社) is a shrine in Japan associated with the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Outer Shrine|ja|外宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Outer Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊受大神宮}} of the [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. This article also covers the {{ill|Shitomi Shrine|lt=Shitomi Shrine|lt=Ōkōchi Shrine|ja|大河内神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkōchi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 志等美神社}}, which shares the same grounds as Shitomi Shrine, as well as the {{ill|Shitomi Shrine|lt=Shitomi Shrine|lt=Uchikake Shrine|ja|打懸神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchikake Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 志等美神社}} and the former village shrine known as {{ill|Shitomi Shrine|lt=Shitomi Shrine|lt=Kami Shrine (Ise)|ja|上社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}, which encompasses all three shrines within its precincts. Among the 125 shrines affiliated with Ise Grand Shrine, only Shitomi, Ōkōchi, and Uchikake shrines hold recognized Ranks in the [[:en:Shinkai (divine rank)|Shinto shrine Ranking system]].Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 342, 355. == Overview == Shitomi Shrine is located at Tsuji Kuru 1-13, Ise City, Mie Prefecture. The precincts are under the jurisdiction of Kami Shrine, with Shitomi, Ōkōchi, and Uchikake shrines positioned at the right side of Kami Shrine's eastern entrance.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 949. The total area of the precincts is approximately 700 tsubo (about 2,314 square meters), of which 406 square meters are occupied by Shitomi, Ōkōchi, and Uchikake shrines.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 343, 356. Shitomi and Ōkōchi shrines share the same fence, facing south, and feature [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] architecture with wooden plank roofs. These shrines do not have offering boxes. == Shitomi Shrine == Shitomi Shrine Ranks 7th among the 16 auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 1, pp. 7, 11–12. Historical records also refer to it as "Shitomi-no-Iba Shrine," with "Shitomi-no-Iba" being the name of the surrounding wilderness.Ise Bunka Sha (2008): p. 53. Some scholars interpret "Shitomi" as derived from the verb "shitomu," meaning "to soak in water" or "to get wet." Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 336. == Ōkōchi Shrine == Although its structure is separate, Ōkōchi Shrine is located within the same fence as Shitomi Shrine.Ise Bunka Sha (2008): p. 54. It Ranks 8th among the 16 auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}}, Ōkōchi Shrine fell into disrepair and later became a component of Kami Shrine as the "Mountain Shrine."Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 950. The current pronunciation of the shrine's name is "Ōkōchi," but historical documents record readings such as "Ōkahachi" and "Ōgafuchi."Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 354. == Uchikake Shrine == Uchikake Shrine Ranks 4th among the eight subsidiary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 1, pp. 13–15. After falling into disrepair during the Sengoku period, it was absorbed into Kami Shrine as "Parallel Shrine" (formerly "Hachioji Shrine"), maintaining its original deity.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 948. == Kami Shrine == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Kami Shrine (上社) |image = [[File:Kami no Shrine, Ise.jpg|250px]] |location = [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], Tsujikuru 1-chome 13 |coordinates = {{coord|34|29|22.3|N|136|41|21.0|E}} |Map = {{location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=29|lat_sec=22.3|lon_deg=136|lon_min=41|lon_sec=21.0|label=Kami Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} |deity = {{ill|Kukunochi|ja|久久能智神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukunochi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ククノチ}}, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu|ja|埴安神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ハニヤス}}, [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama]], and others |Rank = Former Village Shrine (旧村社) |Founded = After the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}} |Main hall = |alternate_name = |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = July 15 |Rituals = {{ill|Onzu-shinji (Head Ritual)|ja|御頭神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onzu-shinji (Head Ritual)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (January 15) }} Kami Shrine is the local [[:en:Ujigami|ujigami]], serving the districts of Nakajima, Tsuji Kuru, Futamata, and Uraguchi. Its treasures include a lion head for [[:en:Lion dance|shishimai]] performances and a sword donated in 1721.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 955. The precincts feature additional shrines such as Inari Shrine, Uegami Shrine dedicated to Sugawara no Michizane, and Kushitama Shrine enshrining Isetsuhiko and Isetsuhime deities, as well as remote {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship sites|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} for other shrines.Ise-Shima Kirari 1000 (2013). === Merged Shrines === Kami Shrine absorbed 13 other shrines in 1909.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, pp. 949. == Worship and Festivals == Shitomi Shrine conducts annual festivals, including the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]], {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}}, [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai Festival]], and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]].Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 343, 355. Kami Shrine's events include the {{ill|Mitō-shinji|ja|御頭神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitō-shinji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on January 15 and its annual festival on July 15. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Ujiyamada City Hall (1929). *Ujiyamada City History*. Vols. 1–2. * Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990). *Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku: Vol. 6, Tōkaidō 1*. Kōgakkan University Press. * Ise Bunka Sha (2008). *O-Ise-san 125 Shrine Pilgrimage*. Ise Bunka Sha. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0605.html Shitomi Shrine] - Jingu Kaikan. * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0606.html Ōkōchi Shrine] - Jingu Kaikan. * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0604.html Uchikake Shrine] - Jingu Kaikan. {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|志等美神社|version=86517017|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] }} [[ja:志等美神社]] [[ja:大河内神社]] [[ja:上社]] [[sn:Shitomi Shrine]] [[sn:hitomi Shrine]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] {{Commons category|Kaminoyashiro, Ise}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17212285]] Wikidata name: Shitomi Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Shitomi Shrine & Ōkōchi Shrine |image = [[File:Shitomi & Okouchi Shrine.jpg|250px]] |location = [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Tsujikuru 1-chome 13 |Latitude = 34 |Latitude minutes = 29 |Latitude seconds = 23.1 |Longitude = 136 |Longitude minutes = 41 |Longitude seconds = 22.1 |Map = Japan Mie |deity = Shitomi Shrine: {{ill|Kukunochi|ja|久久能智神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukunochi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ククノチ|1=Kukunochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10749920}}
Ōkōchi Shrine: {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor)
{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Auxiliary Shrine (Sessha) |Founded = Before [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku era]] 23 (804 CE) |Main hall = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] |alternate_name = |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (October 19) |Rituals = [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]], {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}}, and others }} '''Shitomi Shrine''' (志等美神社) is a shrine in Japan associated with the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Outer Shrine|ja|外宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Outer Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊受大神宮}} of the [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. This article also covers the {{ill|Shitomi Shrine|lt=Shitomi Shrine|lt=Ōkōchi Shrine|ja|大河内神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkōchi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 志等美神社}}, which shares the same grounds as Shitomi Shrine, as well as the {{ill|Shitomi Shrine|lt=Shitomi Shrine|lt=Uchikake Shrine|ja|打懸神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchikake Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 志等美神社}} and the former village shrine known as {{ill|Shitomi Shrine|lt=Shitomi Shrine|lt=Kami Shrine (Ise)|ja|上社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}, which encompasses all three shrines within its precincts. Among the 125 shrines affiliated with Ise Grand Shrine, only Shitomi, Ōkōchi, and Uchikake shrines hold recognized Ranks in the [[:en:Shinkai (divine rank)|Shinto shrine Ranking system]].Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 342, 355. == Overview == Shitomi Shrine is located at Tsuji Kuru 1-13, Ise City, Mie Prefecture. The precincts are under the jurisdiction of Kami Shrine, with Shitomi, Ōkōchi, and Uchikake shrines positioned at the right side of Kami Shrine's eastern entrance.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 949. The total area of the precincts is approximately 700 tsubo (about 2,314 square meters), of which 406 square meters are occupied by Shitomi, Ōkōchi, and Uchikake shrines.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 343, 356. Shitomi and Ōkōchi shrines share the same fence, facing south, and feature [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] architecture with wooden plank roofs. These shrines do not have offering boxes. == Shitomi Shrine == Shitomi Shrine Ranks 7th among the 16 auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 1, pp. 7, 11–12. Historical records also refer to it as "Shitomi-no-Iba Shrine," with "Shitomi-no-Iba" being the name of the surrounding wilderness.Ise Bunka Sha (2008): p. 53. Some scholars interpret "Shitomi" as derived from the verb "shitomu," meaning "to soak in water" or "to get wet." Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 336. == Ōkōchi Shrine == Although its structure is separate, Ōkōchi Shrine is located within the same fence as Shitomi Shrine.Ise Bunka Sha (2008): p. 54. It Ranks 8th among the 16 auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}}, Ōkōchi Shrine fell into disrepair and later became a component of Kami Shrine as the "Mountain Shrine."Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 950. The current pronunciation of the shrine's name is "Ōkōchi," but historical documents record readings such as "Ōkahachi" and "Ōgafuchi."Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 354. == Uchikake Shrine == Uchikake Shrine Ranks 4th among the eight subsidiary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 1, pp. 13–15. After falling into disrepair during the Sengoku period, it was absorbed into Kami Shrine as "Parallel Shrine" (formerly "Hachioji Shrine"), maintaining its original deity.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 948. == Kami Shrine == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Kami Shrine (上社) |image = [[File:Kami no Shrine, Ise.jpg|250px]] |location = [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], Tsujikuru 1-chome 13 |coordinates = {{coord|34|29|22.3|N|136|41|21.0|E}} |Map = {{location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=29|lat_sec=22.3|lon_deg=136|lon_min=41|lon_sec=21.0|label=Kami Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} |deity = {{ill|Kukunochi|ja|久久能智神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukunochi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ククノチ|1=Kukunochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10749920}}, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu|ja|埴安神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ハニヤス}}, [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama]], and others |Rank = Former Village Shrine (旧村社) |Founded = After the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}} |Main hall = |alternate_name = |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = July 15 |Rituals = {{ill|Onzu-shinji (Head Ritual)|ja|御頭神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onzu-shinji (Head Ritual)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (January 15) }} Kami Shrine is the local [[:en:Ujigami|ujigami]], serving the districts of Nakajima, Tsuji Kuru, Futamata, and Uraguchi. Its treasures include a lion head for [[:en:Lion dance|shishimai]] performances and a sword donated in 1721.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 955. The precincts feature additional shrines such as Inari Shrine, Uegami Shrine dedicated to Sugawara no Michizane, and Kushitama Shrine enshrining Isetsuhiko and Isetsuhime deities, as well as remote {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship sites|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} for other shrines.Ise-Shima Kirari 1000 (2013). === Merged Shrines === Kami Shrine absorbed 13 other shrines in 1909.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, pp. 949. == Worship and Festivals == Shitomi Shrine conducts annual festivals, including the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]], {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}}, [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai Festival]], and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]].Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 343, 355. Kami Shrine's events include the {{ill|Mitō-shinji|ja|御頭神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitō-shinji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on January 15 and its annual festival on July 15. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Ujiyamada City Hall (1929). *Ujiyamada City History*. Vols. 1–2. * Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990). *Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku: Vol. 6, Tōkaidō 1*. Kōgakkan University Press. * Ise Bunka Sha (2008). *O-Ise-san 125 Shrine Pilgrimage*. Ise Bunka Sha. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0605.html Shitomi Shrine] - Jingu Kaikan. * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0606.html Ōkōchi Shrine] - Jingu Kaikan. * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0604.html Uchikake Shrine] - Jingu Kaikan. {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|志等美神社|version=86517017|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] }} [[ja:志等美神社]] [[ja:大河内神社]] [[ja:上社]] [[sn:Shitomi Shrine]] [[sn:hitomi Shrine]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] {{Commons category|Kaminoyashiro, Ise}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17212285]] Wikidata name: Shitomi Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Shitomi Shrine & Ōkōchi Shrine |image = [[File:Shitomi & Okouchi Shrine.jpg|250px]] |location = [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Tsujikuru 1-chome 13 |Latitude = 34 |Latitude minutes = 29 |Latitude seconds = 23.1 |Longitude = 136 |Longitude minutes = 41 |Longitude seconds = 22.1 |Map = Japan Mie |deity = Shitomi Shrine: {{ill|Kukunochi|ja|久久能智神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukunochi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ククノチ|1=Kukunochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10749920}}
Ōkōchi Shrine: {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor)
{{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Auxiliary Shrine (Sessha) |Founded = Before [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku era]] 23 (804 CE) |Main hall = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] |alternate_name = |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (October 19) |Rituals = [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]], {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}}, and others }} '''Shitomi Shrine''' (志等美神社) is a shrine in Japan associated with the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Outer Shrine|ja|外宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Outer Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊受大神宮}} of the [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. This article also covers the {{ill|Shitomi Shrine|lt=Shitomi Shrine|lt=Ōkōchi Shrine|ja|大河内神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkōchi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 志等美神社}}, which shares the same grounds as Shitomi Shrine, as well as the {{ill|Shitomi Shrine|lt=Shitomi Shrine|lt=Uchikake Shrine|ja|打懸神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchikake Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 志等美神社}} and the former village shrine known as {{ill|Shitomi Shrine|lt=Shitomi Shrine|lt=Kami Shrine (Ise)|ja|上社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}, which encompasses all three shrines within its precincts. Among the 125 shrines affiliated with Ise Grand Shrine, only Shitomi, Ōkōchi, and Uchikake shrines hold recognized Ranks in the [[:en:Shinkai (divine rank)|Shinto shrine Ranking system]].Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 342, 355. == Overview == Shitomi Shrine is located at Tsuji Kuru 1-13, Ise City, Mie Prefecture. The precincts are under the jurisdiction of Kami Shrine, with Shitomi, Ōkōchi, and Uchikake shrines positioned at the right side of Kami Shrine's eastern entrance.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 949. The total area of the precincts is approximately 700 tsubo (about 2,314 square meters), of which 406 square meters are occupied by Shitomi, Ōkōchi, and Uchikake shrines.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 343, 356. Shitomi and Ōkōchi shrines share the same fence, facing south, and feature [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] architecture with wooden plank roofs. These shrines do not have offering boxes. == Shitomi Shrine == Shitomi Shrine Ranks 7th among the 16 auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 1, pp. 7, 11–12. Historical records also refer to it as "Shitomi-no-Iba Shrine," with "Shitomi-no-Iba" being the name of the surrounding wilderness.Ise Bunka Sha (2008): p. 53. Some scholars interpret "Shitomi" as derived from the verb "shitomu," meaning "to soak in water" or "to get wet." Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 336. == Ōkōchi Shrine == Although its structure is separate, Ōkōchi Shrine is located within the same fence as Shitomi Shrine.Ise Bunka Sha (2008): p. 54. It Ranks 8th among the 16 auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}}, Ōkōchi Shrine fell into disrepair and later became a component of Kami Shrine as the "Mountain Shrine."Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 950. The current pronunciation of the shrine's name is "Ōkōchi," but historical documents record readings such as "Ōkahachi" and "Ōgafuchi."Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 354. == Uchikake Shrine == Uchikake Shrine Ranks 4th among the eight subsidiary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 1, pp. 13–15. After falling into disrepair during the Sengoku period, it was absorbed into Kami Shrine as "Parallel Shrine" (formerly "Hachioji Shrine"), maintaining its original deity.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 948. == Kami Shrine == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Kami Shrine (上社) |image = [[File:Kami no Shrine, Ise.jpg|250px]] |location = [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], Tsujikuru 1-chome 13 |coordinates = {{coord|34|29|22.3|N|136|41|21.0|E}} |Map = {{location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=29|lat_sec=22.3|lon_deg=136|lon_min=41|lon_sec=21.0|label=Kami Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} |deity = {{ill|Kukunochi|ja|久久能智神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukunochi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ククノチ|1=Kukunochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10749920}}, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu|ja|埴安神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ハニヤス}}, [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama]], and others |Rank = Former Village Shrine (旧村社) |Founded = After the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}} |Main hall = |alternate_name = |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = July 15 |Rituals = {{ill|Onzu-shinji (Head Ritual)|ja|御頭神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onzu-shinji (Head Ritual)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (January 15) }} Kami Shrine is the local [[:en:Ujigami|ujigami]], serving the districts of Nakajima, Tsuji Kuru, Futamata, and Uraguchi. Its treasures include a lion head for [[:en:Lion dance|shishimai]] performances and a sword donated in 1721.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 955. The precincts feature additional shrines such as Inari Shrine, Uegami Shrine dedicated to Sugawara no Michizane, and Kushitama Shrine enshrining Isetsuhiko and Isetsuhime deities, as well as remote {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship sites|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} for other shrines.Ise-Shima Kirari 1000 (2013). === Merged Shrines === Kami Shrine absorbed 13 other shrines in 1909.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, pp. 949. == Worship and Festivals == Shitomi Shrine conducts annual festivals, including the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]], {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}}, [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai Festival]], and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]].Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 343, 355. Kami Shrine's events include the {{ill|Mitō-shinji|ja|御頭神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitō-shinji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on January 15 and its annual festival on July 15. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Ujiyamada City Hall (1929). *Ujiyamada City History*. Vols. 1–2. * Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990). *Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku: Vol. 6, Tōkaidō 1*. Kōgakkan University Press. * Ise Bunka Sha (2008). *O-Ise-san 125 Shrine Pilgrimage*. Ise Bunka Sha. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0605.html Shitomi Shrine] - Jingu Kaikan. * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0606.html Ōkōchi Shrine] - Jingu Kaikan. * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0604.html Uchikake Shrine] - Jingu Kaikan. {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|志等美神社|version=86517017|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] }} [[ja:志等美神社]] [[ja:大河内神社]] [[ja:上社]] [[sn:Shitomi Shrine]] [[sn:hitomi Shrine]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] {{Commons category|Kaminoyashiro, Ise}} [[Category:qq]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17212285]] Wikidata name: Shitomi Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Shitomi Shrine & Ōkōchi Shrine |image = [[File:Shitomi & Okouchi Shrine.jpg|250px]] |location = [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Tsujikuru 1-chome 13 |Latitude = 34 |Latitude minutes = 29 |Latitude seconds = 23.1 |Longitude = 136 |Longitude minutes = 41 |Longitude seconds = 22.1 |Map = Japan Mie |deity = Shitomi Shrine: {{ill|Kukunochi|ja|久久能智神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukunochi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ククノチ|1=Kukunochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10749920}}
Ōkōchi Shrine: {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor)
{{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Auxiliary Shrine (Sessha) |Founded = Before [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku era]] 23 (804 CE) |Main hall = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] |alternate_name = |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (October 19) |Rituals = [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]], {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}}, and others }} '''Shitomi Shrine''' (志等美神社) is a shrine in Japan associated with the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Outer Shrine|ja|外宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Outer Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊受大神宮}} of the [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. This article also covers the {{ill|Shitomi Shrine|lt=Shitomi Shrine|lt=Ōkōchi Shrine|ja|大河内神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkōchi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 志等美神社}}, which shares the same grounds as Shitomi Shrine, as well as the {{ill|Shitomi Shrine|lt=Shitomi Shrine|lt=Uchikake Shrine|ja|打懸神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchikake Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 志等美神社}} and the former village shrine known as {{ill|Shitomi Shrine|lt=Shitomi Shrine|lt=Kami Shrine (Ise)|ja|上社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}, which encompasses all three shrines within its precincts. Among the 125 shrines affiliated with Ise Grand Shrine, only Shitomi, Ōkōchi, and Uchikake shrines hold recognized Ranks in the [[:en:Shinkai (divine rank)|Shinto shrine Ranking system]].Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 342, 355. == Overview == Shitomi Shrine is located at Tsuji Kuru 1-13, Ise City, Mie Prefecture. The precincts are under the jurisdiction of Kami Shrine, with Shitomi, Ōkōchi, and Uchikake shrines positioned at the right side of Kami Shrine's eastern entrance.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 949. The total area of the precincts is approximately 700 tsubo (about 2,314 square meters), of which 406 square meters are occupied by Shitomi, Ōkōchi, and Uchikake shrines.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 343, 356. Shitomi and Ōkōchi shrines share the same fence, facing south, and feature [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] architecture with wooden plank roofs. These shrines do not have offering boxes. == Shitomi Shrine == Shitomi Shrine Ranks 7th among the 16 auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 1, pp. 7, 11–12. Historical records also refer to it as "Shitomi-no-Iba Shrine," with "Shitomi-no-Iba" being the name of the surrounding wilderness.Ise Bunka Sha (2008): p. 53. Some scholars interpret "Shitomi" as derived from the verb "shitomu," meaning "to soak in water" or "to get wet." Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 336. == Ōkōchi Shrine == Although its structure is separate, Ōkōchi Shrine is located within the same fence as Shitomi Shrine.Ise Bunka Sha (2008): p. 54. It Ranks 8th among the 16 auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}}, Ōkōchi Shrine fell into disrepair and later became a component of Kami Shrine as the "Mountain Shrine."Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 950. The current pronunciation of the shrine's name is "Ōkōchi," but historical documents record readings such as "Ōkahachi" and "Ōgafuchi."Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 354. == Uchikake Shrine == Uchikake Shrine Ranks 4th among the eight subsidiary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 1, pp. 13–15. After falling into disrepair during the Sengoku period, it was absorbed into Kami Shrine as "Parallel Shrine" (formerly "Hachioji Shrine"), maintaining its original deity.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 948. == Kami Shrine == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Kami Shrine (上社) |image = [[File:Kami no Shrine, Ise.jpg|250px]] |location = [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], Tsujikuru 1-chome 13 |coordinates = {{coord|34|29|22.3|N|136|41|21.0|E}} |Map = {{location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=29|lat_sec=22.3|lon_deg=136|lon_min=41|lon_sec=21.0|label=Kami Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} |deity = {{ill|Kukunochi|ja|久久能智神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukunochi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ククノチ|1=Kukunochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10749920}}, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu|ja|埴安神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ハニヤス}}, [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama]], and others |Rank = Former Village Shrine (旧村社) |Founded = After the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}} |Main hall = |alternate_name = |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = July 15 |Rituals = {{ill|Onzu-shinji (Head Ritual)|ja|御頭神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onzu-shinji (Head Ritual)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (January 15) }} Kami Shrine is the local [[:en:Ujigami|ujigami]], serving the districts of Nakajima, Tsuji Kuru, Futamata, and Uraguchi. Its treasures include a lion head for [[:en:Lion dance|shishimai]] performances and a sword donated in 1721.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 955. The precincts feature additional shrines such as Inari Shrine, Uegami Shrine dedicated to Sugawara no Michizane, and Kushitama Shrine enshrining Isetsuhiko and Isetsuhime deities, as well as remote {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship sites|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} for other shrines.Ise-Shima Kirari 1000 (2013). === Merged Shrines === Kami Shrine absorbed 13 other shrines in 1909.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, pp. 949. == Worship and Festivals == Shitomi Shrine conducts annual festivals, including the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]], {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}}, [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai Festival]], and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]].Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 343, 355. Kami Shrine's events include the {{ill|Mitō-shinji|ja|御頭神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitō-shinji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on January 15 and its annual festival on July 15. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Ujiyamada City Hall (1929). *Ujiyamada City History*. Vols. 1–2. * Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990). *Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku: Vol. 6, Tōkaidō 1*. Kōgakkan University Press. * Ise Bunka Sha (2008). *O-Ise-san 125 Shrine Pilgrimage*. Ise Bunka Sha. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0605.html Shitomi Shrine] - Jingu Kaikan. * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0606.html Ōkōchi Shrine] - Jingu Kaikan. * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0604.html Uchikake Shrine] - Jingu Kaikan. {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|志等美神社|version=86517017|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] }} [[ja:志等美神社]] [[ja:大河内神社]] [[ja:上社]] [[sn:Shitomi Shrine]] [[sn:hitomi Shrine]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] {{Commons category|Kaminoyashiro, Ise}} [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17212285]] Wikidata name: Shitomi Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Shitomi Shrine & Ōkōchi Shrine |image = [[File:Shitomi & Okouchi Shrine.jpg|250px]] |location = [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Tsujikuru 1-chome 13 |Latitude = 34 |Latitude minutes = 29 |Latitude seconds = 23.1 |Longitude = 136 |Longitude minutes = 41 |Longitude seconds = 22.1 |Map = Japan Mie |deity = Shitomi Shrine: {{ill|Kukunochi|ja|久久能智神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukunochi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ククノチ|1=Kukunochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10749920}}
Ōkōchi Shrine: {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor)
{{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Auxiliary Shrine (Sessha) |Founded = Before [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku era]] 23 (804 CE) |Main hall = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] |alternate_name = |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (October 19) |Rituals = [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]], {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}}, and others }} '''Shitomi Shrine''' (志等美神社) is a shrine in Japan associated with the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Outer Shrine|ja|外宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Outer Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊受大神宮}} of the [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. This article also covers the {{ill|Shitomi Shrine|lt=Shitomi Shrine|lt=Ōkōchi Shrine|ja|大河内神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkōchi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 志等美神社}}, which shares the same grounds as Shitomi Shrine, as well as the {{ill|Shitomi Shrine|lt=Shitomi Shrine|lt=Uchikake Shrine|ja|打懸神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchikake Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 志等美神社}} and the former village shrine known as {{ill|Shitomi Shrine|lt=Shitomi Shrine|lt=Kami Shrine (Ise)|ja|上社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}, which encompasses all three shrines within its precincts. Among the 125 shrines affiliated with Ise Grand Shrine, only Shitomi, Ōkōchi, and Uchikake shrines hold recognized Ranks in the [[:en:Shinkai (divine rank)|Shinto shrine Ranking system]].Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 342, 355. == Overview == Shitomi Shrine is located at Tsuji Kuru 1-13, Ise City, Mie Prefecture. The precincts are under the jurisdiction of Kami Shrine, with Shitomi, Ōkōchi, and Uchikake shrines positioned at the right side of Kami Shrine's eastern entrance.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 949. The total area of the precincts is approximately 700 tsubo (about 2,314 square meters), of which 406 square meters are occupied by Shitomi, Ōkōchi, and Uchikake shrines.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 343, 356. Shitomi and Ōkōchi shrines share the same fence, facing south, and feature [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] architecture with wooden plank roofs. These shrines do not have offering boxes. == Shitomi Shrine == Shitomi Shrine Ranks 7th among the 16 auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 1, pp. 7, 11–12. Historical records also refer to it as "Shitomi-no-Iba Shrine," with "Shitomi-no-Iba" being the name of the surrounding wilderness.Ise Bunka Sha (2008): p. 53. Some scholars interpret "Shitomi" as derived from the verb "shitomu," meaning "to soak in water" or "to get wet." Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 336. == Ōkōchi Shrine == Although its structure is separate, Ōkōchi Shrine is located within the same fence as Shitomi Shrine.Ise Bunka Sha (2008): p. 54. It Ranks 8th among the 16 auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}}, Ōkōchi Shrine fell into disrepair and later became a component of Kami Shrine as the "Mountain Shrine."Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 950. The current pronunciation of the shrine's name is "Ōkōchi," but historical documents record readings such as "Ōkahachi" and "Ōgafuchi."Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 354. == Uchikake Shrine == Uchikake Shrine Ranks 4th among the eight subsidiary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 1, pp. 13–15. After falling into disrepair during the Sengoku period, it was absorbed into Kami Shrine as "Parallel Shrine" (formerly "Hachioji Shrine"), maintaining its original deity.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 948. == Kami Shrine == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Kami Shrine (上社) |image = [[File:Kami no Shrine, Ise.jpg|250px]] |location = [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], Tsujikuru 1-chome 13 |coordinates = {{coord|34|29|22.3|N|136|41|21.0|E}} |Map = {{location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=29|lat_sec=22.3|lon_deg=136|lon_min=41|lon_sec=21.0|label=Kami Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} |deity = {{ill|Kukunochi|ja|久久能智神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukunochi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ククノチ|1=Kukunochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10749920}}, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祗神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu|ja|埴安神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ハニヤス}}, [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama]], and others |Rank = Former Village Shrine (旧村社) |Founded = After the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}} |Main hall = |alternate_name = |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = July 15 |Rituals = {{ill|Onzu-shinji (Head Ritual)|ja|御頭神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onzu-shinji (Head Ritual)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (January 15) }} Kami Shrine is the local [[:en:Ujigami|ujigami]], serving the districts of Nakajima, Tsuji Kuru, Futamata, and Uraguchi. Its treasures include a lion head for [[:en:Lion dance|shishimai]] performances and a sword donated in 1721.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, p. 955. The precincts feature additional shrines such as Inari Shrine, Uegami Shrine dedicated to Sugawara no Michizane, and Kushitama Shrine enshrining Isetsuhiko and Isetsuhime deities, as well as remote {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship sites|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} for other shrines.Ise-Shima Kirari 1000 (2013). === Merged Shrines === Kami Shrine absorbed 13 other shrines in 1909.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): Vol. 2, pp. 949. == Worship and Festivals == Shitomi Shrine conducts annual festivals, including the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]], {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}}, [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai Festival]], and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]].Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp. 343, 355. Kami Shrine's events include the {{ill|Mitō-shinji|ja|御頭神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitō-shinji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on January 15 and its annual festival on July 15. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Ujiyamada City Hall (1929). *Ujiyamada City History*. Vols. 1–2. * Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990). *Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku: Vol. 6, Tōkaidō 1*. Kōgakkan University Press. * Ise Bunka Sha (2008). *O-Ise-san 125 Shrine Pilgrimage*. Ise Bunka Sha. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0605.html Shitomi Shrine] - Jingu Kaikan. * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0606.html Ōkōchi Shrine] - Jingu Kaikan. * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0604.html Uchikake Shrine] - Jingu Kaikan. {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|志等美神社|version=86517017|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] }} [[ja:志等美神社]] [[ja:大河内神社]] [[ja:上社]] [[sn:Shitomi Shrine]] [[sn:hitomi Shrine]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] {{Commons category|Kaminoyashiro, Ise}} [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]
